blocks:
سُورة الكهف

Meccan, and it is 110 verses

Surah Al-Kahf

Meccan. Ibn Abbas (ابن عبّاس) said: except for the verse وَاصْبِرْ نَفْسَكَ مَعَ الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ رَبَّهُمْ ** (And be patient with those who supplicate their Lord) for it was revealed in Medina (المدينة) concerning the story of Uyaynah ibn Hisn (عيينة بن حصين). Its number of verses is one hundred and eleven verses.* بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ*** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَنْزَلَ عَلى عَبْدِهِ الْكِتابَ ** (1) All praise belongs to Allah who sent down the Book to His servant, meaning the Qur'an (القرآن). Allah, glory be to Him, taught His servants how they should praise Him for the greatest of His favors upon them, which is the cause (سَبَب) of their salvation. وَلَمْ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ عِوَجاً ** and did not place any crookedness in it. by way of defect (اختلال) in the wording or contradiction (تناقض) in the meaning. And al-ʿiwaj (العوج) with a kasrah (vowel point) is used for meanings (المعاني), like al-ʿawaj (العَوج) with a fathah (vowel point) is used for physical objects (الأعيان).

(٢) قَيِّماً ** He made it straight and moderate; there is no excess (إفراط) in it, nor deficiency (تَفريط). Al-Qummi said: This is an instance of advancement and delay (مقدَّم ومؤخّر), because its meaning is: "Who sent down the Book upon His servant, straight (قَيِّماً), and did not place any crookedness (عِوَجاً) in it." Thus, He advanced one phrase (حَرف) over another phrase (حَرف). لِيُنْذِرَ بَأْساً شَدِيداً ** (that He may warn of a severe punishment from Him) Meaning, that He may warn those who disbelieved of a severe punishment (عذاباً شديداً). مِنْ لَدُنْهُ ** Originating from Him. Al-Ayyashi: The severe punishment (البَأس الشّديد) is Ali (peace be upon him), and he is from the presence of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, having fought His enemy alongside Him. وَيُبَشِّرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ الَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ الصَّالِحاتِ أَنَّ لَهُمْ أَجْراً حَسَناً ** (and give good news to the faithful who do righteous deeds that there is a good reward for them) It is Paradise (الجنّة).

(3) ماكِثِينَ فِيهِ أَبَداً ** (abiding in it forever) without cessation. (4) وَيُنْذِرَ الَّذِينَ قالُوا اتَّخَذَ اللهُ وَلَداً ** (and to warn those who say, 'Allah has taken a son') Al-Qummi means Quraysh, where they said that the angels are the daughters of Allah, and the Jews and the Christians in their saying: عُزَيْرٌ ابْنُ اللهِ ** (Uzayr is the son of Allah) and الْمَسِيحُ ابْنُ اللهِ.** (the Messiah is the son of Allah.)

(٥) ما لَهُمْ (١) بِهِ ** (5) They have no knowledge of it, وبما يقولون مِنْ عِلْمٍ وَلا لِآبائِهِمْ ** and concerning what they say مِنْ عِلْمٍ وَلا لِآبائِهِمْ ** nor did their fathers. الّذين يقلّدونهم فيه بل يقولونه عن جهل مفرط وتوهّم كاذب those whom they imitate concerning it. Rather, they say it out of excessive ignorance and false conjecture (توهّم كاذب). كَبُرَتْ كَلِمَةً ** A monstrous word it is, عظمت مقالتهم هذه في الكفر لما فيها من التشبيه والاشراك This saying of theirs has become immense in disbelief (الكفر) because of the anthropomorphism (التشبيه) and association (الاشراك) it contains. تَخْرُجُ مِنْ أَفْواهِهِمْ issuing from their mouths. استعظام لاجترائهم على إخراجها من أفواههم [This is] an expression of awe (استعظام) at their audacity (اجترائهم) in uttering it from their mouths. إِنْ يَقُولُونَ إِلَّا كَذِباً. They say nothing but a lie.

(٦) فَلَعَلَّكَ باخِعٌ نَفْسَكَ (6) Perhaps you will kill yourself

Al-Qummi narrates from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: killing yourself. عَلى آثارِهِمْ إِنْ لَمْ يُؤْمِنُوا بِهذَا الْحَدِيثِ ** (upon their traces if they do not believe in this discourse) (meaning) with this Quran, out of grief (أَسَفاً). It is related to باخِعٌ نَفْسَكَ ** (Perhaps you will kill yourself). And it is excessive grief and anger. It is as if when they turned away from faith, they departed from him (the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). So He likened him to one whose dearest ones have departed from him, and he grieves over their traces and kills himself out of sorrow (تلهّفاً) over their separation.

(٧) إِنَّا جَعَلْنا ما عَلَى الْأَرْضِ زِينَةً لَها ** (Indeed, We have made whatever is on the earth an adornment for it, so that We may test them [to see] which of them is best in conduct.) That which is suitable to be an adornment for it and for its inhabitants, from its embellishments. لِنَبْلُوَهُمْ أَيُّهُمْ أَحْسَنُ عَمَلاً (so that We may test them [to see] which of them is best in conduct) in dealing with it. And it is he who practices asceticism regarding it, and is not deceived by it, and is content with sufficiency (الكفاف) from it.

(8) وَإِنَّا لَجاعِلُونَ ما عَلَيْها صَعِيداً جُرُزاً (8) and We will surely turn whatever is on it into barren dust. Al-Qummi said: It means ruin (خراباً). And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He said: There will be no vegetation (نبات) upon it. And this is an ascetic detachment (تزهيد) from the world and an alert concerning the objective of good deeds (حسن العمل). And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah did not love the splendor (زهرة) of the world and its immediate enjoyment (عاجلها) for any of His friends (أوليائه), nor did He make them desire it, nor its immediate splendor (عاجل زهرتها) and its apparent beauty (ظاهر بهجتها). Rather, He created the world and created its people so that He might test them therein, [to see] which of them is best in deeds (أحسن عملاً) for his Hereafter.

(٩) أَمْ حَسِبْتَ أَنَّ أَصْحابَ الْكَهْفِ وَالرَّقِيمِ (9) Or do you think that the Companions of the Cave and the Inscription

regarding the preservation of their lives in that state for a long period, كانُوا مِنْ آياتِنا عَجَباً (were a wonder among Our signs?) Al-Qummi says: "We have certainly brought you signs that are more wondrous than that." He said: And they were young men (فِتْيَةٌ) who lived during the interval (الفترة) between Jesus son of Mary (peace be upon him) and Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. As for the Inscription (الرَّقِيمِ), they are two copper tablets (لَوحان) inscribed (مَرقوم), upon which were written the affair of the young men, the affair of their embracing Islam, what King Decius (دَقيانوس) wanted from them, and how their affair and condition transpired.

And Al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They were a people who went missing, and the king of that land wrote their names, the names of their fathers, and their tribes on sheets of lead (رصاص). This is His saying: the Companions of the Cave and the Inscription.

And Al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The reason for the revelation of Surat al-Kahf was that Quraysh sent three men to Najran: Al-Nadr ibn al-Harith ibn Kaladah, Uqbah ibn Abi Mu'ayt, and Al-As ibn Wa'il al-Sahmi, so that they might learn from the Jews and Christians questions to ask the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. So they went out to Najran to the Jewish scholars and asked them. They said: "Ask him about three questions. If he answers them according to what we have, then he is truthful. Then ask him about one single question; if he claims knowledge of it, then he is a liar." They asked: "And what are these questions?" They replied: "Ask him about young men (فِتْيَة) who lived in the former time, who went out, disappeared, and slept. How long did they remain in their sleep until they awoke? What was their number? What was with them besides themselves? And what was their story? And ask him about Moses, when Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded him to follow the scholar (العالم) and learn from him: Who is He? How did he follow Him? And what was his story with Him? And ask him about a traveler (طائف) who traveled the setting place of the sun and its rising place until he reached the barrier (سدّ) of Gog and Magog (يأجوج ومأجُوج): Who is He? And what was His story?" Then they dictated to them the accounts (اخبار) of these three questions and said to them: "If he answers you with what we have dictated to you, then he is truthful. But if he informs you contrary to that, do not believe him." They asked: "So what is the fourth question?" They replied: "Ask him when the Hour (السّاعة) will be established. If he claims knowledge of it, He is a liar, for none knows the establishment of the Hour except Allah, the Blessed and Exalted." So they returned to Mecca and gathered before Abu Talib. They said: "O Abu Talib, your nephew claims that news of the heaven comes to him, and we will ask him questions. If he answers them, we will know that he is truthful, and if he does not inform us, we will know that he is a liar." Abu Talib said: "Ask him whatever occurs to you." So they asked him the three questions. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Tomorrow I will inform you," and he did not make an exception (لم يستثن). So the revelation (الوحي) was withheld from him for forty days, until the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله became distressed, and his companions who had believed in him doubted, and Quraysh rejoiced, mocked, and harmed, and Abu Talib (peace be upon him) grieved. When forty days had passed, Gabriel descended upon him with Surat al-Kahf. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "You have delayed!" He replied: "We are unable to descend except by the permission of Allah, the Exalted." Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: Or do you think O Muhammad, that the Companions of the Cave and the Inscription were a wonder among Our signs?

(9) Or do you think that the Companions of the Cave and the Inscription were a wonder among Our signs?

Then He recounted their story and said: إِذْ أَوَى الْفِتْيَةُ إِلَى الْكَهْفِ فَقالُوا رَبَّنا آتِنا مِنْ لَدُنْكَ رَحْمَةً وَهَيِّئْ لَنا مِنْ أَمْرِنا رَشَداً (10) When the youths took refuge in the Cave, they said, 'Our Lord! Grant us a mercy from Your presence, and dispose our affair for us rightly.'

Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: The Companions of the Cave and the Inscription were in the time of an arrogant, tyrannical king (ملك جبّار عاتٍ) who called the people of his kingdom to the worship of idols (الأصنام). Whoever did not respond to him, he killed. These men were a believing people who worshipped Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. The king had appointed guards (وكلاء) at the gate of the city and did not allow anyone to leave until he had prostrated to the idols. So these men left under the pretext of hunting (الصّيد). This was because they passed by a shepherd (راع) on their way and invited him to their cause, but he did not respond to them. The shepherd had a dog with him, and the dog responded to them and went out with them. Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "None of the beasts (البهائم) will enter Paradise except three: the donkey of Bal'am ibn Ba'ura, the wolf of Joseph, and the dog of the Companions of the Cave." The Companions of the Cave left the city under the pretext of hunting, fleeing from the religion of that king. When evening came, they entered that cave, and the dog was with them. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, cast sleep (النّعاس) upon them, as Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, said: فَضَرَبْنا عَلَى آذانِهِمْ فِي الْكَهْفِ سِنِينَ عَدَداً (11) So We struck their ears in the Cave for a number of years.

So they slept until Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, destroyed the king and the people of his kingdom. That time passed, and another time and other people came. Then they awoke. Some of them said to others: "How long have we slept here?" They looked and saw that the sun had risen high. They said: "We have slept a day or part of a day." Then they said to one of them: "Take this silver coin (الورقة) and enter the city disguised (متنكّراً) so that they do not recognize you. Buy us some food, for if they learn about us and recognize us, they will kill us or force us back into their religion." So that man came and saw the city different from what he had known. He saw people different from those others; he did not recognize them, nor did they recognize his language, nor did he recognize their language. They asked him: "Who are you and where have you come from?" So he informed them. The king of that city went out with his companions, and the man was with them, until they stood at the entrance of the Cave. They began looking into it. Some of them said: "They are three, and their fourth is their dog." And some said: "They are five, and سادِسُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ (their sixth is their dog)." And some said: "They are سَبْعَةٌ وَثامِنُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ (seven, and their eighth is their dog)." And Allah veiled them with a veil of awe (الرّعب), so no one advanced to enter upon them except their companion (the one who went to the city). When he entered upon them, he found them fearful that the companions of Decius (دقيانوس) might have become aware of them. So their companion informed them that they had been sleeping for this long period and that they were a sign (آية) for the people. So they wept and asked Allah to return them to their resting places (مضاجعهم) sleeping as they were. Then the king said: "It is proper that we build a mosque (مسجداً) and visit it, for these are a believing people." They have two turnings (نقلتان) every year: they sleep six months on their right sides and six months on their left sides, and the dog is with them, having stretched out its forelegs (ذراعيه) at the entrance (فناء) of the Cave.

footnote (1) Meaning: Neither these people nor their predecessors have knowledge of this heinous saying; rather, they say that out of ignorance and blind imitation (*تقليد*) without any proof (*حجة*).

(١٠) إِذْ أَوَى الْفِتْيَةُ إِلَى الْكَهْفِ فَقالُوا رَبَّنا آتِنا مِنْ لَدُنْكَ رَحْمَةً ** (When the youths took refuge in the Cave, they said: 'Our Lord! Grant us a mercy from Your presence') which necessitates for us forgiveness, provision, and security from the enemy, وَهَيِّئْ لَنا مِنْ أَمْرِنا ** from the affair (الأمر) upon which we are, which is the separation from the disbelievers (الكفّار), رَشَداً ** whereby we become rightly guided (راشدين), and guided (مهتدين).

(١١) فَضَرَبْنا عَلَى آذانِهِمْ ** (So We struck their ears) Meaning, We struck upon them a veil (حجاباً) that prevented hearing. This means We put them to sleep with a sleep from which sounds would not awaken them. فِي الْكَهْفِ سِنِينَ عَدَداً ** (in the Cave for a number of years) Meaning, years possessing a number (ذوات عدد). (١٢) ثُمَّ بَعَثْناهُمْ ** (Then We raised them up) We awakened them. لِنَعْلَمَ ** (so that We might ascertain) So that Our eternal knowledge (علمنا الأزليّ) may fall upon the known thing (المعلوم) after its occurrence, and so that it may become manifest to them. أَيُّ الْحِزْبَيْنِ ** (which of the two parties) The two differing parties (المختلفين). أَحْصى لِما لَبِثُوا أَمَداً ** (was better at calculating the period they had stayed) Meaning, calculated a duration (أمداً) for the time of their stay, or was more precise (أضبط) concerning it.

(١٣) نَحْنُ نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ نَبَأَهُمْ بِالْحَقِّ إِنَّهُمْ فِتْيَةٌ (13) We recount to you their story truthfully. They were indeed young men In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He said to a man, "What is الفتى (the young man) according to you?" The man replied to him, "The youth (الشابّ)." He said, "No, الفتى is the believer (المؤمن). Indeed, the Companions of the Cave (أصحاب الكهف) were old men (شيوخاً), but Allah named them فتية (young men) because of their faith (إِيمانهم)." And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): A similar narration, except that He said: "They were all middle-aged men (كهولاً)," and He added: "Whoever believes in Allah and is God-wary (اتّقى), he is الفتى." آمَنُوا بِرَبِّهِمْ وَزِدْناهُمْ هُدىً ** who believed in their Lord, and We enhanced their guidance, through divine success (التوفيق) and steadfastness (التثبيت).

(14) وَرَبَطْنا عَلى قُلُوبِهِمْ ** (14) And We fortified their hearts Meaning: We strengthened them and made them firm, until they endured abandoning their homelands and fleeing with their religion to some of the caves (الغيران).

**إِذْ قامُوا فَقالُوا رَبُّنا رَبُّ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ لَنْ نَدْعُوَا مِنْ دُونِهِ إِلهاً لَقَدْ قُلْنا إِذاً شَطَطاً ** (when they stood up and said, 'Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. We will never invoke any god besides Him, for then we would have certainly spoken an outrageous lie.')

(It means) a saying possessing shatat (شطط), that is, possessing distance from the truth, excessive in injustice.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir عليه السّلام: It means injustice (جوراً) against Allah, the Almighty, if we were to say that He has a partner.

I say: They said it secretly, away from the disbelievers, not as the exegetes (المفسّرون) claimed, that they declared it openly before the tyrannical Decius (دقيانوس). And what they did was greater in reward (أجراً).

So, in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Verily, the likeness of Abu Talib is the likeness of the Companions of the Cave (أصحاب الكهف); they concealed faith (الإيمان) and manifested polytheism (الشرك), so Allah gave them their reward twice.

And in it [al-Kafi] and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him عليه السلام: The dissimulation (تقيّة) of no one reached the dissimulation of the Companions of the Cave (أصحاب الكهف), for they used to attend the festivals (الأعياد) and fasten the belts (الزّنانير), so Allah gave them their reward twice.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from him عليه السلام: Verily, the Companions of the Cave (أصحاب الكهف) concealed faith (الإيمان) and manifested disbelief (الكفر), and their reward (أجراً) for manifesting disbelief was greater than their reward for concealing faith. And narrated from him عليه السلام: He mentioned the Companions of the Cave (أصحاب الكهف) and said: "If your people were to burden you with what their people burdened them with..." He was asked: "What did their people burden them with?" He replied: "They burdened them with polytheism (الشرك) in Allah, the Magnificent. So they manifested polytheism to them and concealed faith until relief (الفرج) came to them."

And narrated from him عليه السلام: The Companions of the Cave (أصحاب الكهف) departed without prior knowledge or appointment. When they reached the desert (الصحراء), some of them took covenants and pledges (العهود والمواثيق) from others. So this one took from that one, and that one took from this one. Then they said: "Manifest your affair (أمركم)." So they manifested it, and behold, they were upon a single affair.

And narrated from him عليه السلام: He mentioned the Companions of the Cave (أصحاب الكهف) and said: "They were money changers (صيارفة) of speech, and they were not money changers of dirhams (دراهم)."

هؤُلاءِ قَوْمُنَا اتَّخَذُوا مِنْ دُونِهِ آلِهَةً لَوْ لا يَأْتُونَ ** (These are our people who have taken gods besides Him. Why do they not bring) Why do they not bring عَلَيْهِمْ upon their worship بِسُلْطانٍ بَيِّنٍ ** (an obvious authority concerning them?) with a manifest proof (ببرهان ظاهر). And this is a rebuke (تبكيت) because bringing the argument (الحجّة) for that is impossible (محال). فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ افْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً ** (So who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah?) by attributing partnership (الشّرك) to Him. I say: In this Ayah there is an indication (دلالة) that they were concealing (يسرّون) faith (الايمان), and likewise in what follows it.

(16) وَإِذِ اعْتَزَلْتُمُوهُمْ (And when you withdrew from them) This is an address of some of them to others. وَما يَعْبُدُونَ إِلَّا اللهَ (and what they worship besides Allah) And you withdrew from their worshipped deities or their worship, except Allah. فَأْوُوا إِلَى الْكَهْفِ يَنْشُرْ لَكُمْ رَبُّكُمْ مِنْ رَحْمَتِهِ وَيُهَيِّئْ لَكُمْ مِنْ أَمْرِكُمْ مِرْفَقاً (so take refuge in the Cave; your Lord will unfold His mercy for you and will prepare for you a facility in your affair) That by which you benefit (تَرْتَفِقُونَ بِهِ), meaning you profit from it (تَنْتَفِعُونَ بِهِ). And it was read with the opening of the mīm and the breaking of the fā’. And their certainty (جَزْمُهُمْ) in that was due to the severity of their trust (وُثُوقِهِمْ) in Allah's favor and the strength of their conviction (يَقِينِهِمْ) in Allah.

(١٧) وَتَرَى الشَّمْسَ If you were to see them, إِذا طَلَعَتْ تَزاوَرُ ** (when it rises, inclining away)—it inclines (تَمِيل). And it was recited with the doubling (تَشْدِيد) of the zāy (الزّاي) [تَزَّاوَرُ], and (تَزْوَرُّ) with the doubling of the rāʾ (الراء), like (تَحْمَرُّ)—عَنْ كَهْفِهِمْ ** (from their cave). And its rays do not fall upon them, thereby harming them. Perhaps the cave was facing south (جَنُوبِيّاً). ذاتَ الْيَمِينِ ** (to the right), meaning, the direction of the right side of the cave. وَإِذا غَرَبَتْ تَقْرِضُهُمْ ** (and when it sets, it passes them by)—it cuts them off (تَقْطَعُهُمْ) and severs itself from them (تَصَرَّم عَنْهُمْ)—ذاتَ الشِّمالِ ** (to the left), the direction of the north side of the cave. وَهُمْ فِي فَجْوَةٍ مِنْهُ (while they were in a space within it). They were in a wide space (مُتَّسَع) within the cave, meaning, in the middle (وَسَط), such that the coolness of the breeze (بَرْد النَّسِيم) and the freshness of the air (رَوْح الْهَوَاء) reached them, and they were not harmed by the distress (كَرْب) of the grotto (الْغَار) nor the heat of the sun, neither when it rose nor when it set.

ذلِكَ مِنْ آياتِ اللهِ مَنْ يَهْدِ اللهُ ** (That is one of Allah’s signs. Whomever Allah guides)—by granting success (التَّوْفِيق)—فَهُوَ الْمُهْتَدِ ** (it is he who is rightly guided)—a praise for them. وَمَنْ يُضْلِلْ ** (and whomever He leads astray)—whomever He abandons (يَخْذُلُهُ)—فَلَنْ تَجِدَ لَهُ وَلِيًّا مُرْشِداً ** (you will never find for him any guardian or guide)—one who takes charge of him (يَلِيه) and guides him (يُرْشِدُهُ).

وَتَرَى الشَّمْسَ إِذا طَلَعَتْ تَزاوَرُ عَنْ كَهْفِهِمْ ذاتَ الْيَمِينِ وَإِذا غَرَبَتْ تَقْرِضُهُمْ ذاتَ الشِّمالِ وَهُمْ فِي فَجْوَةٍ مِنْهُ ذلِكَ مِنْ آياتِ اللهِ مَنْ يَهْدِ اللهُ فَهُوَ الْمُهْتَدِ وَمَنْ يُضْلِلْ فَلَنْ تَجِدَ لَهُ وَلِيًّا مُرْشِداً (17) (17) And you see the sun, when it rises, inclining away from their cave to the right, and when it sets, passing them by to the left, while they were in a space within it. That is one of Allah’s signs. Whomever Allah guides, it is he who is rightly guided, and whomever He leads astray, you will never find for him any guardian or guide.

In al-Tawhid and al-Ma'ani, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and he said: "Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, will lead the wrongdoers (الظَّالِمِينَ) astray on the Day of Resurrection from the abode of His generosity (دَار كَرَامَتِهِ), and He will guide the people of faith and righteous deeds (الْعَمَل الصَّالِح) to His Paradise." Just as Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَيُضِلُّ اللهُ الظَّالِمِينَ وَيَفْعَلُ اللهُ ما يَشاءُ ** (And Allah leads the wrongdoers astray, and Allah does whatever He wishes.) And He said: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ يَهْدِيهِمْ رَبُّهُمْ بِإِيمانِهِمْ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهِمُ الْأَنْهارُ فِي جَنَّاتِ النَّعِيمِ.** (Indeed, those who have faith and do righteous deeds—their Lord guides them by their faith. Rivers will run in the Gardens of Bliss beneath them.)

(١٨) وَتَحْسَبُهُمْ (1) أَيْقاظاً (And you would think them awake) Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): You would see their eyes open. وَهُمْ رُقُودٌ ** (while they are asleep) niyam (نيام) (asleep). وَنُقَلِّبُهُمْ ** (And We turned them) in their sleep ذاتَ الْيَمِينِ وَذاتَ الشِّمالِ ** (to the right and to the left) twice every year, as preceded, so that the earth would not consume the parts of their bodies adjacent to it over the length of time. وَكَلْبُهُمْ باسِطٌ ذِراعَيْهِ بِالْوَصِيدِ ** (and their dog stretched its forelegs on the threshold.) bil-fina' (بالفناء) (at the courtyard/threshold). And the narrative (حديث) of the dog has already preceded. لَوِ اطَّلَعْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ لَوَلَّيْتَ مِنْهُمْ فِراراً ** (If you had looked at them, you would have surely turned your back on them in flight,) la-harabta minhum (لهربت منهم) (you would have fled from them). وَلَمُلِئْتَ مِنْهُمْ رُعْباً ** (and you would have surely been filled with awe of them.) khawfan yamla'u sadrak (خوفاً يملأ صَدرك) (a fear that fills your chest). And it was recited (وقرء) as lamulli'ta (لمُلّئت) with tashdid (بالتّشديد) and ru'uban (ورُعَّباً) with tathqil (بالتثقيل). It was said that this was due to the awe (الهيبة) with which Allah clothed them. Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): That was not meant for the Prophet (النّبي); rather, it was meant for the believers (المؤمنون) regarding one another, but it is the state (حالهم) upon which they were.

(١٩) وَكَذلِكَ بَعَثْناهُمْ ** (And thus did We raise them up) Just as We caused them to sleep as a sign, We raised them up as a sign of the perfection of Our power, so that they might question one another so that some of them might ask others, and thus recognize their condition and what Allah had done to them, thereby increasing their certainty upon their certainty, and gaining insight through it regarding the matter of the Resurrection (البَعث). قالَ قائِلٌ مِنْهُمْ كَمْ لَبِثْتُمْ قالُوا لَبِثْنا يَوْماً أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍ ** (A speaker among them said, 'How long have you stayed?' They said, 'We have stayed a day or part of a day.') based on their predominant assumption derived from customary sleep. قالُوا رَبُّكُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِما لَبِثْتُمْ (They said, 'Your Lord knows best how long you have stayed.') It is said they said that when they saw the length of their nails and hair. Then, when they knew that the matter was confusing (ملتبس) and they had no way to know it, they turned to what concerned them and said: فَابْعَثُوا أَحَدَكُمْ بِوَرِقِكُمْ هذِهِ (So send one of you with this money of yours) It was read with the sukūn on the rāʾ (الراء). إِلَى الْمَدِينَةِ (to the city) And al-waraq (الورق) is silver (money). فَلْيَنْظُرْ أَيُّها أَزْكى طَعاماً (and let him observe which of them has the purest food) Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) says: "Which of them has the most pleasant food (أطيب طعاماً)." And in al-Mahasin, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): Azka ta'āman (ازكى طعاماً) is dates (التّمر).

I say: It is inferred from this that the explicit pronoun (البارز) in which of them refers back to the foods (الاطعمة), not to the city (meaning its inhabitants), as the majority (الجمهور) understood. فَلْيَأْتِكُمْ بِرِزْقٍ مِنْهُ وَلْيَتَلَطَّفْ (and bring you some provision from it, and let him be careful) meaning, let him undertake the effort of gentleness (اللّطف) in concealing himself (التخفّي) and disguising himself (التنكّر) so that he is not recognized, as preceded in the narration (حديث) of Al-Qummi (القمّيّ). And His saying explains it: وَلا يُشْعِرَنَّ بِكُمْ أَحَداً. (and let no one become aware of you.)

(٢٠) إِنَّهُمْ إِنْ يَظْهَرُوا عَلَيْكُمْ ** (20) If they prevail over you, If they gain victory over you, meaning the people of the city (المدينة), يَرْجُمُوكُمْ they will kill you by stoning (الرّجم), and it is the most wicked form of killing, أَوْ يُعِيدُوكُمْ فِي مِلَّتِهِمْ or bring you back into their creed, and they will make you return to it unwillingly, **وَلَنْ تُفْلِحُوا إِذاً أَبَداً ** and then you will never be felicitous. if you enter their creed.

(٢١) وَكَذلِكَ أَعْثَرْنا عَلَيْهِمْ ** (21) And thus did We let them be discovered And just as We caused them to sleep, We resurrected them so that their insight might increase. We made the people of their city aware of them. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): And they are those who went to the door of the Cave. لِيَعْلَمُوا ** so that those whom We made aware of their condition might know أَنَّ وَعْدَ اللهِ ** concerning the Resurrection (البَعْث) حَقٌّ وَأَنَّ السَّاعَةَ ** is bound to come لا رَيْبَ فِيها ** that it is inevitable, because their condition in their sleep and awakening is like the condition of one who dies and is resurrected. And in the Prophetic Hadith (الحديث النّبوي): "Just as you sleep, you awaken, and just as you die, you are resurrected." And in another Hadith: "Sleep is the brother of death." And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a Hadith: "Many people who died have returned to the world, among them the Companions of the Cave. Allah caused them to die for three hundred years and nine, then He resurrected them in the time of a people who denied the Resurrection (البعث), so that He might cut off their argument, show them His power, and so that they might know that the Resurrection (البعث) is true." إِذْ يَتَنازَعُونَ ** We made them discovered when they were disputing among themselves concerning their affair (أَمْرَهُمْ), which was the affair of their religion. Some of them were saying that souls would be resurrected disembodied, and others were saying that both (soul and body) would be resurrected together, so that the disagreement might be lifted and it might become clear that both are resurrected together—this is what was said. And in the Hadith of al-Ihtijaj, there was an allusion to this. And it was said that their affair (أَمْرَهُمْ) means the affair of the young men when He caused them to die a second time. Some of them were saying they died, and others were saying they slept, like their sleep the first time. This was previously mentioned in the Hadith of al-Qummi (القمّيّ). And however it was, فَقالُوا ابْنُوا عَلَيْهِمْ بُنْياناً ** when He caused them to die a second time. رَبُّهُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِهِمْ ** (This is) an interjection. قالَ الَّذِينَ غَلَبُوا عَلى أَمْرِهِمْ ** from the Muslims and their king, لَنَتَّخِذَنَّ عَلَيْهِمْ مَسْجِداً ** where the Muslims may pray and seek blessing (يتبرّكون) through their location.

footnote (١) Meaning, if you had seen them, you would have thought them awake, while they were *ruqūd* (*رقود*), meaning asleep (*نائمون*).

(٢٢) سَيَقُولُونَ ثَلاثَةٌ رابِعُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ ** (22) They will say, 'Three, their fourth being their dog,' Meaning the people of the city (أهل المدينة) and their king, as preceded in the narration (حديث) of al-Qummi (القمّيّ). And it is said, rather, He means by them those who delved into their story during the era of our Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, from the People of the Book (اهل الكتاب) and the believers (المؤمنين). وَيَقُولُونَ خَمْسَةٌ سادِسُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ رَجْماً بِالْغَيْبِ ** and they will say, 'Five, their sixth being their dog,' guessing at the unseen. They cast a guess (يرمون رمياً) with the hidden news (بالخبر الخفيّ). And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [said]: "Guessing at the unseen (ظنّاً بالغيب) is what they ask them for a legal opinion (ما يستفتونهم)." وَيَقُولُونَ سَبْعَةٌ وَثامِنُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ قُلْ رَبِّي أَعْلَمُ بِعِدَّتِهِمْ ما يَعْلَمُهُمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلٌ and they will say, 'Seven, their eighth being their dog.' Say, 'My Lord knows best their number. None knows them except a few.' The general populace (العامّة) narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): "They are seven, and their eighth is their dog." What is narrated through the path of the select (الخاصّة) in Rawdat al-Wa'izin (روضة الواعظين) from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) indicates this: "Twenty-seven men will emerge with the Qa'im (القائم) from behind the Ka'bah (الكعبة): fifteen from the people of Moses who were يَهْدُونَ بِالْحَقِّ وَبِهِ يَعْدِلُونَ (guide by the truth and do justice thereby), and seven from the People of the Cave (اهل الكَهف), and Yusha' ibn Nun (يُوشع بن نون), and Salman (سَلمان), and Abu Dujanah al-Ansari (أبو دجانة الانصاري), and al-Miqdad (المقداد), and Malik al-Ashtar (مالك الأشتر). They will be before Him as helpers (انصاراً) and rulers (حكّاماً)." فَلا تُمارِ فِيهِمْ إِلَّا مِراءً ظاهِراً ** So do not dispute concerning them, except in an outward dispute, So do not argue with the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) concerning the matter of the young men (الفتية) except with an outward argument (جدالاً ظاهراً) that is not deeply involved (غير متعمّق فيه). This means that you should narrate to them what has been revealed to you, without declaring them ignorant (تجهيل لهُمْ) or refuting them. وَلا تَسْتَفْتِ فِيهِمْ مِنْهُمْ أَحَداً. and do not ask any of them for a fatwa concerning them. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: Meaning, He says: "What We have narrated to you concerning their affair is sufficient for you, so do not ask anyone from the People of the Book (اهل الكتاب) about them."

(٢٣) وَلا تَقُولَنَّ لِشَيْءٍ تعزم عليه إِنِّي فاعِلٌ ذلِكَ غَداً. (23) Do not say about anything, 'I will indeed do that tomorrow,'

(٢٤) إِلَّا أَنْ يَشاءَ اللهُ الّا متلبّساً بمشيّته قائلاً إن شاء الله تعالى وَاذْكُرْ رَبَّكَ إِذا نَسِيتَ يعني إذا نسيت الاستثناء فاستثن إذا ذكرت. (24) without [adding], 'If Allah wills.' And remember your Lord when you forget, except being connected to His Will, saying: If Allah wills, the Exalted. Meaning: if you forgot the exception (الاستثناء), then make the exception (فاستثن) when you remember.

وفي الجوامع عن الصادق عليه السلام : ما لم ينقطع الكلام. In al-Jawami', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): [You may do so] as long as the speech has not been cut off.

وفي الكافي عنه عليه السلام : أنّه سئل عن قوله تعالى وَاذْكُرْ رَبَّكَ إِذا نَسِيتَ قال ذلك في اليمين إذا قلت والله لا افعل كذا وكذا فإذا ذكرت انّك لم تستثن فقل إن شاء الله. In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about the Almighty's saying: وَاذْكُرْ رَبَّكَ إِذا نَسِيتَ (And remember your Lord when you forget). He said: That is concerning the oath (اليمين). If you say, "By Allah, I will not do such-and-such," and then you remember that you did not make the exception (تستثن), then say: إن شاء الله (If Allah wills).

والعيّاشي عنه عليه السلام : ما في معناه في عدّة روايات. And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him) that which is similar in meaning in several narrations (روايات).

وفي الكافي والعيّاشي عنه عليه السلام قال قال أمير المؤمنين عليه السلام : الاستثناء في اليمين متى ما ذكر وإن كان بعد أربعين صباحاً ثمّ تلا هذه الآية. In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: The exception (الاستثناء) in the oath (اليمين) [can be made] whenever one remembers, even if it is after forty mornings. Then he recited this Ayah.

وفي الفقيه عن الصّادق عليه السلام : للعبد أن يستثني ما بينه وبين أربعين يوماً إذا نسي انّ رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم أتاه ناس من اليهود فسألوه عن أشياء فقال لهم تعالوا غداً أحدثكم ولم يستثن فاحتبس جبرئيل عنه أربعين يوماً ثمّ أتاه فقال وَلا تَقُولَنَّ لِشَيْءٍ الآية. In al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is permissible for the servant to make the exception (يستثني) within the period between him and forty days if he forgot. [This is because] people from the Jews came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and asked him about certain things, and he said to them: "Come tomorrow, and I will inform you," but he did not make the exception (يستثن). So, Gabriel was withheld from him for forty days. Then he came to him and said: وَلا تَقُولَنَّ لِشَيْءٍ (Do not say about anything...) [to the end of] the Ayah.

والعيّاشي عنه عن أبيه عن أمير المؤمنين عليه السّلام : مثله. And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him), from his father, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): the like of it.

وفي الكافي عن الباقر عليه السلام : في قول الله عزّ وجلّ وَلَقَدْ عَهِدْنا إِلى آدَمَ مِنْ قَبْلُ فَنَسِيَ وَلَمْ نَجِدْ لَهُ عَزْماً انّ الله عزّ وجلّ لمّا قال لآدم وزوجته لا تَقْرَبا هذِهِ الشَّجَرَةَ ولا تأكلا منها فقالا نعم يا ربّنا لا نقربها ولا نأكل منها ولم يستثنيا في قولهما نعم فوكلهما الله في ذلك الى أنفسهما والى ذكرهما قال وقد قال الله عزّ وجلّ لنبيّه في الكتاب وَلا تَقُولَنَّ لِشَيْءٍ إِنِّي فاعِلٌ ذلِكَ غَداً إِلَّا أَنْ يَشاءَ اللهُ ان لا افعله فتسبق مشيّة الله في ان لا افعله فلا اقدر على ان افعله فلذلك قال الله عزّ وجلّ وَاذْكُرْ رَبَّكَ إِذا نَسِيتَ أي استثن مشيّة الله في فعلك. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَلَقَدْ عَهِدْنا إِلى آدَمَ مِنْ قَبْلُ فَنَسِيَ وَلَمْ نَجِدْ لَهُ عَزْماً (We certainly made a covenant with Adam before, but he forgot, and We did not find any resolve in him). [He said]: When Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said to Adam and his wife: لا تَقْرَبا هذِهِ الشَّجَرَةَ (Do not approach this tree) and do not eat from it, they said: "Yes, O our Lord, we will not approach it nor eat from it," but they did not make the exception (يستثنيا) in their saying "Yes." So, Allah entrusted them in that matter to themselves and to their remembrance. He said: And Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has indeed said to His Prophet in the Book: وَلا تَقُولَنَّ لِشَيْءٍ إِنِّي فاعِلٌ ذلِكَ غَداً إِلَّا أَنْ يَشاءَ اللهُ (Do not say about anything, 'I will indeed do that tomorrow,' without [adding], 'If Allah wills,') that I should not do it, so that the Will (مشيّة) of Allah precedes that I should not do it, and thus I am unable to do it. For that reason, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَاذْكُرْ رَبَّكَ إِذا نَسِيتَ (And remember your Lord when you forget), meaning: make the exception (استثن) of Allah's Will (مشيّة) in your action.

والعيّاشي عنه عليه السلام قال : قال الله عزّ وجلّ وَلا تَقُولَنَ إلى آخر الحديث كما ذكر في الكافي. And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَلا تَقُولَنَ [Do not say...] to the end of the narration, as mentioned in al-Kafi.

وعنه عليه السّلام : انّ آدم لمّا أسكنه الله الجنّة فقال له يا آدم لا تقرب هذه الشجرة فقال نعم ولم يستثن فأمر الله تعالى نبيّه صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم فقال وَلا تَقُولَنَّ لِشَيْءٍ إِنِّي فاعِلٌ الى قوله إِذا نَسِيتَ ولو بَعد سنة. And narrated from him (peace be upon him): When Allah settled Adam in Paradise, He said to him: "O Adam, do not approach this tree." He said: "Yes," but he did not make the exception (يستثن). So, Allah, the Exalted, commanded His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: وَلا تَقُولَنَّ لِشَيْءٍ إِنِّي فاعِلٌ (Do not say about anything, 'I will indeed do...') up to His saying: إِذا نَسِيتَ (when you forget), even if it is after a year.

قال في المجمع : الوجه فيه انّه إذا استثنى بعد النسيان فانه يحصل له ثواب المستثنى من غير أنّ يؤثّر الاستثناء بعد انفصال الكلام في الكلام وإبطال الحنث وسقوط الكفّارة في اليمين. He said in al-Majma': The rationale (الوجه) for this is that when he makes the exception (استثنى) after forgetting, he obtains the reward (ثواب) of the one who made the exception (المستثنى), without the exception (الاستثناء) after the separation of speech having an effect on the speech itself, or invalidating the breach of oath (الحنث), or causing the expiation (الكفّارة) for the oath (اليمين) to be dropped.

وفي الكافي عن الصّادق عليه السلام : انّه امر بكتاب في حاجة فكتب ثمّ عرض عليه ولم يكن فيه استثناء فقال كيف رجوتم ان يتمّ هذا وليس فيه استثناء انظروا كلّ موضع لا يكون فيه استثناء فاستثنوا فيه وفي التهذيب ما يقرب منه وزاد ثمّ دعا بالدّوات فقال الحق فيه إن شاء الله فألحق فيه في كلّ موضع إن شاء الله In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He commanded that a letter be written concerning a need, and it was written. Then it was presented to him, and there was no exception (استثناء) in it. He said: "How do you hope that this will be completed when there is no exception (استثناء) in it? Look at every place where there is no exception (استثناء) and make an exception (فاستثنوا) in it." And in al-Tahdhib is that which is close to it, and it added: Then he called for the inkwell (الدّواة) and said: "Insert إن شاء الله (If Allah wills) into it." So, إن شاء الله (If Allah wills) was inserted into it in every place.

وَقُلْ عَسى أَنْ يَهْدِيَنِ رَبِّي لِأَقْرَبَ مِنْ هذا رَشْداً قيل اي يهديني لشيء آخر بدل هذا المنسي اقرب منه رشدا وادنى خيراً ومنفعة أو لما هو ظهر دلالة على انّي نبيء من نبأ أصحاب الكهف. And say, 'Maybe my Lord will guide me to what is nearer to rectitude than this.' It was said: meaning, He guides me to something else instead of this forgotten matter, which is nearer to rectitude (رشداً) than it, and closer in goodness (خيراً) and benefit (منفعة), or to that which is a clearer indication that I am a Prophet (نبيء) regarding the news (نبأ) of the People of the Cave (أصحاب الكهف).

(٢٥) وَلَبِثُوا فِي كَهْفِهِمْ ثَلاثَ مِائَةٍ سِنِينَ ** (25) And they remained in their cave three hundred years And it was read with the genitive construction (بالإضافة). and added nine** meaning three hundred and nine.

(٢٦) قُلِ اللهُ أَعْلَمُ بِما لَبِثُوا ** (26) Say, 'Allah knows best what time they remained.' regarding the duration of their stay, better than those among the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) who differed concerning it. The truth is what Allah informed, and this is what is mentioned in al-Majma': It is narrated that a Jew asked Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) about the duration of their stay, and he informed him of what is in the Quran. The Jew said: "We find three hundred in our book." Ali (peace be upon him) said: "That is according to the solar years (سني الشمس), and this is according to the lunar years (سني القمر)."

And al-Qummi connected this to the first report which narrated that they (the people discussing the sleepers) said: "Three, their dog being the fourth," and then He said: And they remained in their cave three hundred years and added nine (25). This is a narration (حكاية) about them, though its wording is a statement (خبر). The proof that it is a narration about them is His saying: Say, 'Allah knows best what time they remained. To Him belongs the unseen of the heavens and the earth.' He is specialized in His knowledge. How clearly He sees and how well He hears! "How clearly He sees and how well He hears!" (فما أبصره لو أسمعه) is mentioned in the form of astonishment (صيغة التعجّب) to indicate that His matter concerning perception (الإدراك) is outside the scope of the perception of every seer and hearer, since nothing screens Him, and nothing, whether subtle (لطيف) or dense (كثيف), small or large, hidden (خفيّ) or manifest (جليّ), differs in relation to Him. They have, besides Him, no guardian, They, meaning the inhabitants of the heavens and the earth, besides Him, no guardian, who manages their affairs, and He does not associate anyone in His judgment in His decree, anyone of them. And it was read with the tā' (ت) and the jazm (جزم).

(٢٧) وَاتْلُ ما أُوحِيَ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ كِتابِ رَبِّكَ لا مُبَدِّلَ لِكَلِماتِهِ وَلَنْ تَجِدَ مِنْ دُونِهِ مُلْتَحَداً ** (Recite what has been revealed to you from the Book of your Lord. There is no changing His words, and you will never find any refuge besides Him.) وَاتْلُ ما أُوحِيَ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ كِتابِ رَبِّكَ ** (Recite what has been revealed to you from the Book of your Lord) means: from the Quran. لا مُبَدِّلَ لِكَلِماتِهِ وَلَنْ تَجِدَ مِنْ دُونِهِ مُلْتَحَداً ** (There is no changing His words, and you will never find any refuge besides Him) means: a shelter (ملتجأ) and a haven (موئلاً). It is said: التحد الى كذا (he sought refuge in such-and-such) when he inclined towards it.

(٢٨) ** وَاصْبِرْ نَفْسَكَ مَعَ الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ رَبَّهُمْ بِالْغَداةِ وَالْعَشِيِ يُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَهُ وَلا تَعْدُ عَيْناكَ عَنْهُمْ تُرِيدُ زِينَةَ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا وَلا تُطِعْ مَنْ أَغْفَلْنا قَلْبَهُ عَنْ ذِكْرِنا وَاتَّبَعَ هَواهُ وَكانَ أَمْرُهُ فُرُطاً ** (Keep your soul in patience with those who supplicate their Lord morning and evening, desiring His face. Do not turn your eyes away from them, desiring the glitter of the life of the world. And do not obey him whose heart We have made oblivious to Our remembrance, and who follows his own desires, and whose affair is excessive.) ** وَاصْبِرْ نَفْسَكَ ** (Keep your soul in patience) means: restrain it (احبسها). مَعَ الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ رَبَّهُمْ بِالْغَداةِ وَالْعَشِيِ ** (with those who supplicate their Lord morning and evening) means: at the two ends of the day, or during the totality of their times. Al-Ayyashi narrated from both of them (peace be upon them) (عنهما عليهما السّلام): He only meant by these two times (بهما) the prayer (الصلاة). And it was recited بالغدوة (in the morning). يُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَهُ (desiring His face) means: His pleasure (رضاه) and obedience (طاعته). وَلا تَعْدُ عَيْناكَ عَنْهُمْ (Do not turn your eyes away from them) means: let not your gaze pass beyond them to others among the people of the world (أبناء الدنيا). تُرِيدُ زِينَةَ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ** (desiring the glitter of the life of the world) means: by sitting with the people of wealth (أهل الغنى). وَلا تُطِعْ مَنْ أَغْفَلْنا قَلْبَهُ عَنْ ذِكْرِنا ** (And do not obey him whose heart We have made oblivious to Our remembrance) means: through abandonment (بالخذلَان). وَاتَّبَعَ هَواهُ وَكانَ أَمْرُهُ فُرُطاً (and who follows his own desires, and whose affair is excessive) means: extravagance (إفراطاً), transgression of the limit (تجاوزاً للحدّ), and casting the truth behind his back (نبذاً للحق وراء ظهره). Al-Qummi: It was revealed concerning Salman al-Farisi (may Allah be pleased with him) (رض). He wore a cloak (كساء) in which his food would be kept, and it was his inner garment (دثاره) and his outer garment (رداؤه). It was a woolen cloak (كساء من صوف). Then Uyaynah ibn Hisn entered upon the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) while Salman was with him. Uyaynah was bothered by the smell of Salman's cloak, as he had sweated in it, and it was a day of intense heat, so he sweated in the cloak. He said: "O Messenger of Allah, when we enter upon you, remove this man and his party (وحزبه) from your presence. When we leave, you may admit whomever you wish." Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: وَلا تُطِعْ مَنْ أَغْفَلْنا قَلْبَهُ ** (And do not obey him whose heart We have made oblivious to Our remembrance) [the rest of] the Ayah. This man was Uyaynah ibn Hisn ibn Hudhayfah ibn Badr al-Fazari (الْفَزَارِيّ). And in al-Majma': It was revealed concerning Salman, Abu Dharr, Suhayb, Khabbab, and others among the poor companions of the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace). This was because the people whose hearts were to be reconciled (المؤلّفة قلوبهم)—Uyaynah ibn Hisn, Al-Aqra' ibn Habis, and their likes (وذووهم)—came to the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) and said: "O Messenger of Allah, if you sit at the front of the gathering and remove these men and the smell of their sweat (روايح صنانهم) from us (and they were wearing woolen cloaks (جباب), which is the plural of جبة), we will sit with you and take knowledge from you. Nothing prevents us from entering upon you except these men." When the Ayah was revealed, the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) stood up seeking them out. He found them in the back of the mosque remembering Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. He said: "Praise be to Allah Who did not cause me to die until He commanded me to keep my soul in patience with men from my Ummah (أمّتي)—with them is the life-giver (المحيي) and with them is the death-bringer (الممات)."

(٢٩) وَقُلِ الْحَقُّ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ ** (29) Say, 'The truth is from your Lord.' It is the truth from your Lord, or the truth is what comes from the direction of Allah, not what desire (الهوى) dictates. فَمَنْ شاءَ فَلْيُؤْمِنْ وَمَنْ شاءَ فَلْيَكْفُرْ ** So let anyone who wishes believe, and let anyone who wishes disbelieve. Thus, nothing remains except your choice for yourselves of whatever you wish, whether taking the path of salvation or the path of perdition (الهلاك).

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: [This is] a threat (وعيد). إِنَّا أَعْتَدْنا ** Indeed, We have prepared We have prepared (أعددنا) and made ready (هيّئنا). لِلظَّالِمِينَ ناراً أَحاطَ بِهِمْ سُرادِقُها ** for the wrongdoers a Fire whose curtains encompass them. Its pavilion (فسطاطها). That which surrounds them of the Fire is likened to it. وَإِنْ يَسْتَغِيثُوا ** And if they call for aid, Due to thirst (العَطش). يُغاثُوا بِماءٍ كَالْمُهْلِ ** they will be aided with water like molten copper, Like the dregs (دُرديّ) of oil, and it is said, like molten copper (النّحاس المذاب). يَشْوِي الْوُجُوهَ which will scald the faces. When one approaches it to drink due to the extremity of its heat. بِئْسَ الشَّرابُ What a terrible drink, The المُهل (molten substance). وَساءَتْ ** and what a bad The Fire. مُرْتَفَقاً ** resting place! A resting place (متّكئاً) derived from المرفق (elbow/support), and this is similar to His saying: وَحَسُنَتْ مُرْتَفَقاً. (and what an excellent resting place!)

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Gabriel (peace be upon him) descended with this Ayah thus: وَقُلِ الْحَقُّ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ (Say, 'The truth is from your Lord.') concerning the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him), فَمَنْ شاءَ فَلْيُؤْمِنْ وَمَنْ شاءَ فَلْيَكْفُرْ إِنَّا أَعْتَدْنا لِلظَّالِمِينَ (So let anyone who wishes believe, and let anyone who wishes disbelieve. Indeed, We have prepared for the wrongdoers) against the Family of Muhammad (آل محمّد) a Fire.

And in al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): [It is] similar to it. And he said: The المُهل is that which remains at the bottom (اصل) of boiling oil (الزّيت المغليِّ).

(٣٠) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ إِنَّا لا نُضِيعُ أَجْرَ مَنْ أَحْسَنَ عَمَلاً (30) Indeed, those who have faith and do righteous deeds—We do not waste the reward of those who are good in deeds. (٣١) أُولئِكَ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهِمُ الْأَنْهارُ يُحَلَّوْنَ فِيها مِنْ أَساوِرَ مِنْ ذَهَبٍ وَيَلْبَسُونَ ثِياباً خُضْراً مِنْ سُنْدُسٍ وَإِسْتَبْرَقٍ ** (31) For such there will be Gardens of Eden, with streams running in them. They will be adorned therein with bracelets of gold, and wear green garments of silk and brocade. (This refers to) what is fine (رقّ) of the silk brocade (الدّيباج) and what is coarse (غلظ) of it. مُتَّكِئِينَ فِيها عَلَى الْأَرائِكِ ** (reclining therein on couches) — upon the thrones (السّرر), as is the manner (هيئة) of those who enjoy bliss (المتنعّمين). Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): الْأَرائِكِ ** (the couches) are the thrones (السّرر) upon which are the canopies (الحجال). نِعْمَ الثَّوابُ ** (What an excellent reward) is Paradise (الجنّة) and its bliss (نعيمها). وَحَسُنَتْ الأرائك مُرْتَفَقاً. (and what a good resting place are the couches!) I say: And it is as if the green garments (الثياب الخضر) are an allusion (كناية) to their ideal (المثاليّة) bodies of the intermediate realm (البرزخيّة), which are intermediate between the blackness (سواد) of this world (هذا العالم) and the whiteness (بياض) of the higher world (العالم الأعلى). For greenness (الخضرة) is composed of blackness (سواد) and whiteness (بياض). And the fineness (الرّقة) and the coarseness (الغلظة) are two allusions (كنايتان) to their difference (تفاوتهما) in the degrees of subtlety (مراتب اللطافة).

(٣٢) وَاضْرِبْ لَهُمْ مَثَلاً ** (32) And propound to them a parable for the disbeliever and the believer. رَجُلَيْنِ ** (in the state of) two men. Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning a man who had two large, magnificent gardens (بستانان) with abundant fruits, just as Allah, the Mighty and the Majestic, related. In them were date palms, cultivation (زرع), and water. He had a poor neighbor, and the rich man boasted over the poor man. جَعَلْنا لِأَحَدِهِما جَنَّتَيْنِ ** two gardens (بستانين) مِنْ أَعْنابٍ ** of grapevines (من الكروم) وَحَفَفْناهُما بِنَخْلٍ ** and We made the date palms surrounding them وَجَعَلْنا بَيْنَهُما ** in their midst زَرْعاً ** so that each of them would combine sustenance (الأقوات) and fruits in a beautiful form and elegant arrangement.

(٣٣) كِلْتَا الْجَنَّتَيْنِ آتَتْ أُكُلَها ** (33) Both gardens yielded their produce its fruit (ثمرها) وَلَمْ تَظْلِمْ مِنْهُ ** and did not diminish from its produce (أكلها) شَيْئاً ** and did not fail in the least. as happens in other gardens, for the fruits are completed in one year and diminished in another year, usually (غالباً). وَفَجَّرْنا خِلالَهُما نَهَراً and We made a river gush forth in their midst. so that their irrigation (شربهما) might endure and their splendor (بهاؤهما) might increase.

(٣٤) وَكانَ لَهُ ثَمَرٌ (34) And he had abundant produce. types of wealth besides the two gardens, from the produce (ثمر) of his wealth when it multiplied. And it was read with two fathahs (بفتحتين) and with the dammah on the thā' and the sukūn on the mīm (بضمّ الثاء وسكون الميم).

فَقالَ لِصاحِبِهِ وَهُوَ يُحاوِرُهُ ** (34) So he said to his companion, while he was conversing with him, meaning he was exchanging speech with him, derived from (حار) when he returned.

أَنَا أَكْثَرُ مِنْكَ مالاً وَأَعَزُّ نَفَراً ** (34) 'I am greater than you in wealth and mightier in respect of men.' meaning children and helpers (أعوانا).

(٣٥) وَدَخَلَ جَنَّتَهُ ** (35) And he entered his garden, with his companion, circling with him within it and boasting to him about it. وَهُوَ ظالِمٌ لِنَفْسِهِ ** while he was unjust to himself. harming himself through his arrogance (عجبه) and his disbelief (كفره). قالَ ما أَظُنُّ أَنْ تَبِيدَ ** He said, 'I do not think that it will perish that it will perish (ان تفنى). هذِهِ ** this meaning this garden. أَبَداً ** ever.' due to the length of his hope, the persistence of his heedlessness (غفلته), and his being deceived by the respite (مهلته) granted to him.

(٣٦) وَما أَظُنُّ السَّاعَةَ قائِمَةً ** occurring (كائنة) وَلَئِنْ رُدِدْتُ إِلى رَبِّي ** by resurrection (بالبَعث), as you claimed, لَأَجِدَنَّ خَيْراً مِنْها مُنْقَلَباً a return (مرجعاً) and an outcome (وعاقبة). And it was recited (وقرئ) minhumā (منهما). (36) 'and I do not think the Hour will ever occur. And if I am returned to my Lord, I will surely find a better resort than it.'

(٣٧) قالَ لَهُ صاحِبُهُ وَهُوَ يُحاوِرُهُ أَكَفَرْتَ بِالَّذِي خَلَقَكَ مِنْ تُرابٍ ** for He is the origin (أصل) of your matter (مادّتك) and the matter (مادّة) of your origin (أصلك) ثُمَّ مِنْ نُطْفَةٍ ** for it is your proximate matter (مادّتك القريبة) ثُمَّ سَوَّاكَ رَجُلاً ** then He proportioned you (عدلك) and perfected you (كمّلك) as a human (إنساناً), a male (ذكراً), reaching the stage of men (بالغاً مبلغ الرّجال). (37) His companion said to him, as he was conversing with him, 'Did you disbelieve in Him who created you from dust, then from a drop of semen, then fashioned you into a man?

(٣٨) لكِنَّا هُوَ اللهُ رَبِّي ** (38) But He is Allah, my Lord, and I do not ascribe any partner to my Lord. Its origin is لكن أنا (but I). And it was recited with the alif (الألف) in both conjunction (الوصل) and pause (الوقف). وَلا أُشْرِكُ بِرَبِّي أَحَداً.**

(٣٩) وَلَوْ لا إِذْ دَخَلْتَ جَنَّتَكَ قُلْتَ ** (39) Why, when you entered your garden, did you not say, ‘What Allah has willed [has come to pass]! There is no power except with Allah!’ If you see me as being less than you in wealth and children, And why did you not say upon entering it: ما شاءَ اللهُ ** (What Allah has willed [has come to pass]!)? What Allah wills exists (كائن), as an acknowledgment that it and what is within it are by the Will (مشيّة) of Allah; if Allah wills, He preserves it, and if He wills, He destroys it. لا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللهِ ** (There is no power except with Allah!) And you said, "There is no power except with Allah," as an admission of incapacity (العجز) on your part and power (القدرة) belonging to Allah, and that what was made easy for you regarding its cultivation (عمارتها) and management (تدبيرها) is by His aid (معونته) and His decrees (أقداره). إِنْ تَرَنِ أَنَا أَقَلَّ مِنْكَ مالاً وَوَلَداً.**

(٤٠) فَعَسى رَبِّي أَنْ يُؤْتِيَنِ خَيْراً مِنْ جَنَّتِكَ ** (40) So maybe my Lord will give me something better than your garden in this world (الدّنيا) or in the Hereafter (الآخرة) due to my faith (إِيماني). وَيُرْسِلَ عَلَيْها (and send upon it) — meaning, upon your garden (جنّتك) due to your disbelief (كفرك) — حُسْباناً مِنَ السَّماءِ (a reckoning from the sky), missiles (مرامى) of His punishment (عذابه), such as a thunderbolt (صاعقة) or the like. And it was said that it means calculation (الحساب), and what is intended by it is the decree (التقدير) of its destruction (تخريبها). فَتُصْبِحَ صَعِيداً زَلَقاً ** so that it becomes a barren slippery ground a smooth land (ارضاً مَلْساء) upon which one slips, due to the uprooting (استيصال) of its plants (نباتها) and trees (أشجارها). Al-Qummi said: burnt (محترقا).

(٤١) أَوْ يُصْبِحَ ماؤُها غَوْراً sunk deep into the earth (غائراً في الأرض) فَلَنْ تَسْتَطِيعَ لَهُ طَلَباً (41) (Or its water will sink into the ground, so that you will not be able to seek it out.) (٤٢) وَأُحِيطَ بِثَمَرِهِ And He destroyed his wealth (أمواله), just as his companion had warned him. [The phrase أُحِيطَ بِهِ (was encompassed by it)] is like when the enemy encompasses someone, for when the enemy encompasses him, he overcomes him, and when he overcomes him, he destroys him. Its parallel (نظيره) is أَتَى (came/befell) when He destroyed it, according to al-Majma' (في المجمع). And in the narrative (الخبر): Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), sent fire upon it, so He destroyed it, and its water sank deep (غار ماؤها). فَأَصْبَحَ يُقَلِّبُ كَفَّيْهِ turning them back to front (ظهراً لبطن), out of regret (تلهّفاً) and sorrow (تحسّراً) عَلى ما أَنْفَقَ فِيها وَهِيَ خاوِيَةٌ fallen (ساقطة) عَلى عُرُوشِها Meaning: the trellises (عروش) of his grapevines (كرومها) fell onto the ground, and the grapevines fell on top of them. وَيَقُولُ يا لَيْتَنِي لَمْ أُشْرِكْ بِرَبِّي أَحَداً It is as if he remembered the admonition (موعظة) of his brother (companion) and knew that it was due to his polytheism (شركه). So, he wished that he had not been a polytheist, and then Allah would not have destroyed his garden (بستانه). (42) (And his produce was encompassed [by ruin], and he began to wring his hands for what he had spent on it, and it was fallen upon its trellises, and he said, 'I wish I had not associated anyone with my Lord!')

The Almighty's saying (43): وَلَمْ تَكُنْ لَهُ فِئَةٌ (43) And he had no company) And it was read with the yāʾ (ي) يَنْصُرُونَهُ by repelling the destruction or averting the ruin مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ** مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ (from besides Allah) for He is the One capable of that alone. وَما كانَ مُنْتَصِراً ** وَما كانَ مُنْتَصِراً (and he was not one who could defend himself) withstanding Allah's vengeance from him.

(44) هُنالِكَ ** [Means] in that station (المقام) and that state (الحال). And it was said: [It refers to] the Hereafter. الْوَلايَةُ لِلَّهِ الْحَقِ ** [Means] the help (النّصرة) belongs to Him alone; no one other than Him has power over it. And it was recited with kasrah (بالكَسر), meaning the authority (السّلطان) and the dominion (الملك). And الْحَقُّ was recited with raf‘ (بالرّفع) as an adjective (صفة) for الولاية. هُوَ خَيْرٌ ثَواباً وَخَيْرٌ عُقْباً (1) (He is best in reward and best in consequence) [Meaning, He is best in reward and consequence] for His friends (لأوليائه). And عُقْباً was recited with sukūn (بالسّكون).

(٤٥) وَاضْرِبْ لَهُمْ مَثَلَ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا (45) (45) Draw for them the parable of the life of the world: that which resembles it in its bloom and the swiftness of its passing away. كَماءٍ it is like water it is like water أَنْزَلْناهُ مِنَ السَّماءِ فَاخْتَلَطَ بِهِ نَباتُ الْأَرْضِ which We send down from the sky, and the earth’s vegetation mingles with it. it became dense because of it and intertwined until some parts of it mixed with others. فَأَصْبَحَ هَشِيماً Then it becomes chaff crushed (مهشوماً), broken. تَذْرُوهُ الرِّياحُ which the winds scatter. the winds scatter it, so it becomes as if it had never existed. وَكانَ اللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ And Allah is Omnipotent over everything. concerning everything, including creation (الإنشاء) and annihilation (الافناء). مُقْتَدِراً Omnipotent.

(٤٦) الْمالُ وَالْبَنُونَ زِينَةُ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ** (46) Wealth and children are the adornment of the life of the world and it perishes soon وَالْباقِياتُ الصَّالِحاتُ ** (but the lasting righteous deeds)—and the deeds of goodness and piety whose fruit remains forever and ever— خَيْرٌ عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ (are better with your Lord) than wealth and children ثَواباً (as a reward) and return (عَائِدَة) **وَخَيْرٌ أَمَلاً ** (and better in hope) because its doer attains in the Hereafter what he hoped for through them in the world.

In al-Tahdhib and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): If Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said الْمالُ وَالْبَنُونَ زِينَةُ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا (Wealth and children are the adornment of the life of the world), then the eight rak'ahs that the servant prays at the end of the night are the adornment of the Hereafter.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Indeed, الْباقِياتُ الصَّالِحاتُ ** (the lasting righteous deeds) are the prayer (الصلاة), so safeguard it.

And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): They are the five obligatory prayers (الصلوات الخمس).

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Indeed, among الْباقِياتُ الصَّالِحاتُ ** (the lasting righteous deeds) is standing for the night prayer (صلاة اللّيل).

And Ibn 'Uqdah narrated from him (peace be upon him): He said to Husayn ibn 'Abd al-Rahman: "Do not belittle our affection (مودّتنا), for it is among الْباقِياتُ الصَّالِحاتُ. (the lasting righteous deeds)."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Take your shield (جُنَّتكم)." They said: "O Messenger of Allah, has an enemy arrived?" He said: "No, but take your shield from the Fire." They said: "Then with what shall we take our shield, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "سُبْحانَ اللهِ وَالْحَمْدُ لله ولا إِلهَ الَّا اللهُ والله أكبر (Glory be to Allah, and praise be to Allah, and there is no god but Allah, and Allah is the Greatest). For they will come on the Day of Resurrection, and they will have things preceding them and things following them, and they are الْباقِياتُ الصَّالِحاتُ. (the lasting righteous deeds)."

And in al-Majma', through the route of the general populace (العامّة): the like of it.

And al-Qummi said: الْباقِياتُ الصَّالِحاتُ ** (the lasting righteous deeds) are سُبْحَان الله والْحَمْدُ لِلّهِ ولا الهَ الَّا اللهُ واللهُ اكبَرُ (Glory be to Allah, and praise be to Allah, and there is no god but Allah, and Allah is the Greatest), mentioned in Surat Maryam.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم passed by a man planting a sapling in his wall (حائط). He stopped by him and said: "Shall I not guide you to a planting that is firmer in root, quicker in ripening, sweeter in fruit, and more lasting?" He said: "Yes, guide me, O Messenger of Allah." He said: "When you wake up in the morning and when you reach the evening, say: سُبحان الله والحمد لله ولا إله الا الله والله أكبر (Glory be to Allah, and praise be to Allah, and there is no god but Allah, and Allah is the Greatest). For if you say it, you will have ten trees in Paradise of various kinds of fruit for every glorification (تسبيحة), and they are among الْباقِياتُ الصَّالِحاتُ (the lasting righteous deeds)."

footnote (١) أي عاقبة طاعته خير من عاقبة طاعة غيره. (1) Meaning, the consequence (عاقبة) of obedience to Him is better than the consequence (عاقبة) of obedience to others.

(٤٧) وَيَوْمَ نُسَيِّرُ الْجِبالَ ** (47) And the day We shall set the mountains moving We shall set them moving in the atmosphere and make them scattered dust (هباءً منبثّاً). And it was recited with the tāʾ (ت) and built for the passive voice (i.e., the mountains are set moving). وَتَرَى الْأَرْضَ بارِزَةً ** and you will see the earth exposed, manifest (بادية), having emerged (برزت) from beneath the mountains, with nothing upon it to conceal it. وَحَشَرْناهُمْ ** and We shall muster them, and We shall gather them to the Standing Place (الموقف). فَلَمْ نُغادِرْ so We shall not leave behind (or: so We shall not forsake) مِنْهُمْ أَحَداً.** leaving out none of them.

(٤٨) وَعُرِضُوا عَلى رَبِّكَ صَفًّا ** (48) They will be presented before your Lord in ranks: You see their gathering just as every single one of them sees, no one screening anyone else. In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They will be, on that Day, one hundred and twenty thousand ranks across the breadth of the earth. لَقَدْ جِئْتُمُونا كَما خَلَقْناكُمْ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ** (You have certainly come to Us as We created you the first time.) Meaning, it will be said to them: We have certainly resurrected you just as We originated you the first time. Or the meaning is: You have certainly come to Us naked, with nothing of wealth or children with you, due to His saying: وَلَقَدْ جِئْتُمُونا فُرادى (And certainly you have come to Us alone), as preceded in Surat al-An'am. بَلْ زَعَمْتُمْ أَلَّنْ نَجْعَلَ لَكُمْ مَوْعِداً ** (48) Rather you claimed that We would not appoint a tryst for you.) [Meaning:] a time for the fulfillment of the promise of resurrection (البعث) and gathering (النشور), and that the prophets lied to you concerning it.

(٤٩) وَوُضِعَ الْكِتابُ (49) And the Book is set up. the scrolls of deeds فَتَرَى الْمُجْرِمِينَ مُشْفِقِينَ مِمَّا فِيهِ Then you will see the guilty fearful of what is in it, afraid of the sins وَيَقُولُونَ يا وَيْلَتَنا and they will say, 'Woe to us! they call upon their destruction ما لِهذَا الْكِتابِ What is the matter with this Book? expressing astonishment at its affair لا يُغادِرُ صَغِيرَةً It omits neither a small one a small matter (هنة صغيرة) وَلا كَبِيرَةً nor a big one, an expression denoting the encompassing of all (deeds) إِلَّا أَحْصاها but has recorded it!' except that He counted it and recorded it وَوَجَدُوا ما عَمِلُوا حاضِراً They will find whatever they have done present. written in the scrolls وَلا يَظْلِمُ رَبُّكَ أَحَداً And your Lord does not wrong anyone. by writing down against him what he did not do, or by not diminishing the reward of a good-doer, or by increasing the punishment of an evil-doer.

Al-Qummi said: They will find all that they did written down.

And Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When the Day of Resurrection comes, the book (of deeds) will be handed to the person, then it will be said: "Read." So he will read what is in it and remember it. There is no glance, nor word, nor movement of a foot, except that he will remember it, as if he performed it at that very hour. That is why they said: يا وَيْلَتَنا (Woe to us!)—the rest of the Ayah.

(٥٠) وَإِذْ قُلْنا لِلْمَلائِكَةِ اسْجُدُوا لِآدَمَ فَسَجَدُوا إِلَّا إِبْلِيسَ ** (50) And when We said to the angels, ‘Prostrate before Adam,’ they prostrated, but not Iblis. Its interpretation (تفسيره) (or: its mention (ذكره), according to another manuscript variant (خ ل)) has already preceded in Surat al-Baqarah. It is said that He repeated it in various places because it serves as an introduction to the matters intended for clarification in those contexts. And thus is every repetition in the Quran. كانَ مِنَ الْجِنِّ فَفَسَقَ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِ ** He was one of the jinn, so he transgressed the command of his Lord. So he departed from His command by abandoning the prostration. أَفَتَتَّخِذُونَهُ ** Will you then take him After what occurred from him, will you take him وَذُرِّيَّتَهُ أَوْلِياءَ مِنْ دُونِي ** and his progeny as guardians instead of Me, and substitute them for Me, so you obey them instead of obeying Me? **وَهُمْ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ بِئْسَ لِلظَّالِمِينَ بَدَلاً ** while they are your enemies? Evil is the substitute for the wrongdoers! Iblis and his progeny are a substitute for Allah.

(٥١) ما أَشْهَدْتُهُمْ خَلْقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (51) I did not make them witness the creation of the heavens and the earth, I did not make Iblis and his progeny present at the creation of the heavens and the earth, seeking strength (اعتضاداً) through them, وَلا خَلْقَ أَنْفُسِهِمْ ** nor the creation of their own souls, nor did I make some of them present at the creation of others, وَما كُنْتُ مُتَّخِذَ الْمُضِلِّينَ عَضُداً ** nor was I one to take the astray as assistants. meaning, helpers (أعواناً). So why do you take them as partners with Me in worship or obedience? Or the meaning is: I did not make the polytheists (المشركين) witness the creation of that, nor did I single them out with knowledge that others do not know, such that if they were to believe, people would follow them, as they claim. So, do not pay attention to their saying, hoping for their support for the religion, for it is not proper for Me to seek strength (اعتضد) for My religion through the astray (المضلّين). And this is supported by the reading of those who read (وما كنت) [in the second person] in the address to the Messenger.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: "O Allah, strengthen Islam through Umar ibn al-Khattab, and through Abu Jahl and Hisham." Then Allah revealed this verse, meaning the two of them [Abu Jahl and Hisham].

I say : It is possible to reconcile between the two interpretations by generalizing the devils (الشّياطين) to include both Jinn and humans (الانس).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Jawad (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, has always been unique in His Oneness (بوحدانيّته). Then He created Muhammad, Ali, and Fatimah (peace be upon them). They remained for a thousand ages (ألف دهر). Then He created all things, and He made them witness their creation, and He made obedience to them incumbent upon [all things], and He entrusted their affair to them." (The rest of) the narrative (الحديث).

(٥٢) وَيَوْمَ يَقُولُ ** (52) And the day He says, meaning Allah says, and it was read with the nūn (وقرء بالنّون).

نادُوا شُرَكائِيَ الَّذِينَ زَعَمْتُمْ ** 'Call upon My partners whom you claimed [to be My partners].' that they are My partners. He attributed the partners to Himself based on their claim, as a reprimand (توبيخاً) to them. And what is meant is what was worshipped besides Him: jinn, humans, and others.

فَدَعَوْهُمْ ** So they will call upon them, meaning they called upon them for rescue (للاغاثة).

فَلَمْ يَسْتَجِيبُوا لَهُمْ ** but they will not respond to them,** so they did not rescue them.

وَجَعَلْنا بَيْنَهُمْ ** and We shall place between them** between the disbelievers and their deities

مَوْبِقاً ** a place of perdition. a place of destruction (مهلكاً) in which they share, and it is a valley among the valleys of Hell. Al-Qummi said: meaning a barrier (سترا). And it was said: al-bayn (البين) is in the sense of connection (الوصل), meaning We made their connection in the world a destruction (هلاكاً) on the Day of Resurrection.

(٥٣) وَرَأَى الْمُجْرِمُونَ النَّارَ فَظَنُّوا ** (53) The guilty will see the Fire, and realize The interpretation of فَظَنُّوا (and realize) is: فأيقنوا (so they became certain). أَنَّهُمْ مُوَاقِعُوهَا ** that they are going to fall into it, The interpretation of مُوَاقِعُوهَا (going to fall into it) is: مخالطوها واقعون فيها (they will mingle with it, falling into it). وَلَمْ يَجِدُوا عَنْهَا مَصْرِفاً ** and they will find no escape from it. The interpretation of مَصْرِفاً** (escape) is: معدلاً (a turning away/deviation).

In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): It means they became certain that they would enter it.

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from him (peace be upon him): And sometimes the realization (ظنّ) of the disbelievers is certainty (يقيناً). That is His saying: وَرَأَى الْمُجْرِمُونَ النَّارَ —the Ayah—meaning: they became certain that they would fall into it.

(54) وَلَقَدْ صَرَّفْنا فِي هذَا الْقُرْآنِ لِلنَّاسِ مِنْ كُلِّ مَثَلٍ وَكانَ الْإِنْسانُ أَكْثَرَ شَيْءٍ ** from whom argumentation (الجدل) proceeds جَدَلاً ** disputation (خصومة) with falsehood (بالباطل). (54) We have certainly variegated in this Quran every kind of parable for the people, but man is the most argumentative of all things. (55) وَما مَنَعَ النَّاسَ أَنْ يُؤْمِنُوا إِذْ جاءَهُمُ الْهُدى وَيَسْتَغْفِرُوا رَبَّهُمْ ** from their sins (من ذنوبهم) إِلَّا أَنْ تَأْتِيَهُمْ سُنَّةُ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** which is destruction (الإهلاك) and eradication (الاستيصال) أَوْ يَأْتِيَهُمُ الْعَذابُ ** the punishment of the Hereafter (عذاب الآخرة) قُبُلاً ** visibly (عياناً). It was recited with two dammahs (بضمّتين). (55) Nothing prevents the people from believing when the guidance comes to them and seeking forgiveness of their Lord, except that the precedent of the former generations should come upon them, or that the punishment should come upon them face to face.

(٥٦) وَما نُرْسِلُ الْمُرْسَلِينَ إِلَّا مُبَشِّرِينَ وَمُنْذِرِينَ وَيُجادِلُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِالْباطِلِ ** (56) We do not send the apostles except as bearers of good news and as warners. Yet the faithless dispute with falsehood such as their saying to the prophets: "You are nothing but humans like us," and "Had Allah willed, He would have sent down angels," and their proposing signs (الآيات) after the appearance of the miracles (المعجزات), and other than that. لِيُدْحِضُوا بِهِ ** so that they may remove the truth (الْحَقَ) from its established place and invalidate it through the dispute. وَاتَّخَذُوا آياتِي وَما أُنْذِرُوا هُزُواً (and they took My signs and what they were warned of as a mockery) meaning: ridicule (استهزاء).

(٥٧) وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنْ ذُكِّرَ بِآياتِ رَبِّهِ ** (57) And who is a greater wrongdoer than him who is reminded of the signs of his Lord meaning the Quran, فَأَعْرَضَ عَنْها ** then turns away from them so he does not reflect upon them nor remember them, وَنَسِيَ ما قَدَّمَتْ يَداهُ ** and forgets what his hands have sent ahead? of disbelief (الكفر) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي), so he does not contemplate their consequence. إِنَّا جَعَلْنا عَلى قُلُوبِهِمْ أَكِنَّةً Indeed, We have cast veils over their hearts This is a justification (تعليل) for their turning away and their forgetting, [stating] that their hearts are sealed (مطبوع على قلوبهم), أَنْ يَفْقَهُوهُ ** lest they understand it, [These veils] prevent them from understanding it. The masculine singular pronoun (الضّمير وإفراده) refers to the meaning (للمعنى) [of the reminder/Quran]. وَفِي آذانِهِمْ وَقْراً ** and a deafness in their ears. [This deafness] prevents them from hearing it with the proper hearing (حقّ استماعه). وَإِنْ تَدْعُهُمْ إِلَى الْهُدى فَلَنْ يَهْتَدُوا إِذاً أَبَداً And if you invite them to guidance, they will never be guided then. Thus, guidance is absolutely not possible from them, neither through realization (تحقيقاً) because they do not understand (يفقهون), nor through imitation (تقليداً) because they do not hear (يسمعون).

**(٥٨) وَرَبُّكَ الْغَفُورُ ذُو الرَّحْمَةِ لَوْ يُؤاخِذُهُمْ بِما كَسَبُوا لَعَجَّلَ لَهُمُ الْعَذابَ ** (58) Your Lord is the All-forgiving, the Possessor of mercy. If He were to take them to task for what they have earned, He would have surely hastened the punishment for them.

So He does not take them to task immediately, despite their deserving the punishment. بَلْ لَهُمْ مَوْعِدٌ ** (But they have an appointment) meaning the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). And it was said: the Day of Badr (يوم بَدر). لَنْ يَجِدُوا مِنْ دُونِهِ مَوْئِلاً ** (They will not find any refuge besides Him) meaning a refuge (ملجأ) and a place of safety (منجى).

(59) وَتِلْكَ الْقُرى أَهْلَكْناهُمْ لَمَّا ظَلَمُوا وَجَعَلْنا لِمَهْلِكِهِمْ مَوْعِداً (٥٩) (59) And those towns—We destroyed them when they did wrong, and We appointed a tryst for their destruction.

وَتِلْكَ الْقُرى (And those towns) refers to the towns of 'Ad, Thamud, and their likes (اضرابهم). أَهْلَكْناهُمْ لَمَّا ظَلَمُوا ** (We destroyed them when they did wrong) like the wrongdoing (ظلم) of Quraysh through denial (التكذيب), disputation (المراء), and various types of disobedience (المعاصي). وَجَعَلْنا لِمَهْلِكِهِمْ (And We appointed for their destruction) meaning, for their destruction (لإِهلاكهم). And it was read with a kasrah on the lām and with a fathah on the mīm and the lām, meaning for their destruction (لهلاكهم). مَوْعِداً ** (a tryst) meaning, a known time (وقتاً معلوماً) from which they cannot delay an hour nor advance. So let them take heed from them and not be deceived by the delay of the punishment from them.

Al-Qummi said: meaning the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة), they will enter the Fire.

(60) وَإِذْ قالَ مُوسى لِفَتاهُ (60) And when Mūsā said to his young companion

In al-Ikmal, al-Ayyashi, and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He is Yusha' ibn Nun. It is said that he is Yusha' ibn Nun ibn Afra'im ibn Yusuf, for he used to serve him and follow him, and for that reason, He (Musa) named him his young companion (فتاه).

لا أَبْرَحُ ** I will not stop traveling حَتَّى أَبْلُغَ مَجْمَعَ الْبَحْرَيْنِ ** (until I reach the junction of the two seas), which is the meeting point of the seas of Persia (فارس) and Rome (الرّوم), and it is the place where Musa was promised to meet al-Khidr أَوْ أَمْضِيَ حُقُباً ** (or I go on for ages), meaning: or I travel for a long time (زماناً طويلاً).

لا أَبْرَحُ حَتَّى أَبْلُغَ مَجْمَعَ الْبَحْرَيْنِ أَوْ أَمْضِيَ حُقُباً (1) I will not cease until I reach the junction of the two seas, (2) or I go on for ages.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The age (الحقب) is eighty years.

And al-Qummi (narrated): When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم informed Quraysh about the story of the Companions of the Cave (أصحاب الكهف), they said: "Inform us about the learned man (العالم) whom Allah commanded Musa to follow, and what his story is." So Allah, the Almighty, the Majestic, sent down: **وَإِذْ قالَ مُوسى لِفَتاهُ ** (And when Mūsā said to his young companion).

He said: The reason for that was that when كَلَّمَ اللهُ مُوسى تَكْلِيماً ** (and Allah spoke to Mūsā directly), He sent down the Tablets upon him, and in them was what Allah the Almighty said: وَكَتَبْنا لَهُ فِي الْأَلْواحِ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ مَوْعِظَةً وَتَفْصِيلاً لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ ** (And We wrote for him in the Tablets an admonition concerning all things and a detailed explanation of all things). Musa returned to the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل), ascended the pulpit (المنبر), and informed them that Allah had sent down the Torah (التوراة) upon him and had spoken to him. He said to himself: "Allah has not created any creature more knowledgeable than me." So Allah revealed to Jibra'il (جبرئيل): "Overtake Musa, for he has perished (هلك), and inform him that at the junction of the two seas, near the rock (الصّخرة), there is a man more knowledgeable than you. So travel to him and learn from his knowledge." Jibra'il descended upon Musa and informed him. Musa humbled himself (ذلّ) inwardly and knew that he had erred, and fear (الرّعب) entered him. He said to his successor (وصيّه), Yusha': "Allah has commanded me to follow a man at the junction of the two seas and learn from him." So Yusha' took a salted fish (حوتاً مملوحاً) as provision, and they departed.

And in al-Ilal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): [A narration] close to the beginning (صَدر) of this tradition (الحديث).

And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him), who said: While Musa was sitting among a gathering (ملإ) of the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل), a man said to him: "I do not see anyone more knowledgeable of Allah than you." Musa said: "I do not see [anyone]." So Allah revealed to Him: "Rather, My servant al-Khidr." So he asked for the way (السّبيل) to him. The sign (آية) for him was the fish, that he would miss it, and his affair was as Allah recounted.
