(61) فَلَمَّا بَلَغا مَجْمَعَ بَيْنِهِما نَسِيا حُوتَهُما ** (61) So when they reached the junction of the two [seas], they forgot their fish. They left it due to their distraction from it or its departure from them. فَاتَّخَذَ سَبِيلَهُ ** meaning the fish فِي الْبَحْرِ سَرَباً ** a path (مسلكاً).

Al-Qummi: When they departed and reached that place, they found a man lying on his back, and they did not recognize him. Then the successor (وصيّ) of Moses brought out the fish, washed it with water, placed it on the rock, and they departed, forgetting the fish. That water was the Water of Life (ماء الحيوان), so the fish came back to life (حيى) and entered the water. Moses (peace be upon him) and Joshua (يوشع) went on with him until they became exhausted (عييا).

Al-Ayyashi mentioned the story of the fish in two other ways. One time, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He roasted it, then carried it in a large basket (مكتل) (1). Then they set off walking until they came upon an old man lying down, with his staff placed beside him, and upon him was a cloak (كساء): if he covered his head, his feet emerged, and if he covered his feet, his head emerged. He said: Then Moses (peace be upon him) stood up to pray and said to Joshua (يوشع): "Guard [this] for me." He said: Then a drop fell from the sky into the large basket (المكتل), and the fish thrashed about, then it began to leap from the large basket (المكتل) into the sea. This is His saying: فَاتَّخَذَ سَبِيلَهُ فِي الْبَحْرِ سَرَباً (and it took its way into the sea, slipping away). He said: Then a bird came and landed on the shore of the sea (ساحل البحر [or شاطئ in another variant]) then it inserted its beak and said: "O Moses, what you have taken from the knowledge of your Lord is no more than what the back of my beak carries from the entirety of the sea." (The rest of the narrative is omitted here, indicated by الحديث).

And another time, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): When the affair of Moses (peace be upon him) was as it was, he was given a large basket (مكتل) containing a salted fish. It was said to him: "This will guide you to your companion at the junction of the two seas, near a rock where there is a spring (عين) from which nothing dead is touched except that it comes alive. It is called the Spring of Life (عين الحياة)." So they set off until they reached the rock. The youth went to wash the fish in the spring, and it thrashed about in his hand until it scratched him and slipped away from him, and the youth forgot it.

In al-Ikmal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), that He said to one of the Jews who had asked Him about several matters: "As for your question concerning the first spring (عين) that gushed forth upon the face of the earth, the Jews claim that it is the spring in Jerusalem (بيت المقدّس) beneath the rock, but they have lied. It is the Spring of Life (عين الحيوان) where Moses and his youth arrived, and he washed the salted fish in it, and it came back to life. There is no dead thing that water touches except that it comes alive. Al-Khidr was in the vanguard (مقدّمة) of Dhul-Qarnayn, seeking the Spring of Life (عين الحياة), and he found it and drank from it, but Dhul-Qarnayn did not find it."

(1) Al-Miktal (المكتل) — similar to the pattern of minbar (منبر) —: The large basket (الزنبيل الكبير).

(62) فَلَمَّا جاوَزا When they had passed beyond the junction of the two seas (مجمع البحرين), قالَ لِفَتاهُ آتِنا غَداءَنا he said to his young companion, 'Bring us our lunch, that which we eat for lunch (ما نتغدى به). لَقَدْ لَقِينا مِنْ سَفَرِنا هذا نَصَباً We have certainly encountered fatigue on this journey of ours,' meaning, toil (عناء). Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He only became weary when the time passed.

(٦٣) قالَ أَرَأَيْتَ ** meaning: Did you see what afflicted me (ما دَهَانِي)? إِذْ أَوَيْنا إِلَى الصَّخْرَةِ فَإِنِّي نَسِيتُ الْحُوتَ ** meaning: I left it and lost it, or I forgot to mention its state and what I saw of it to you. وَما أَنْسانِيهُ ** (It was recited (وقرئ) with the ḍammah on the hā’ (بِضَمِّ الْهَاءِ)) إِلَّا الشَّيْطانُ أَنْ أَذْكُرَهُ meaning: And none but Satan made me forget its mention (ذِكْرَهُ). وَاتَّخَذَ سَبِيلَهُ فِي الْبَحْرِ عَجَباً.** (63) He said, 'Did you see, when we took shelter at the rock? Indeed, I forgot the fish. And none but Satan made me forget it, so that I would not mention it. And it took its way into the sea miraculously.'

(٦٤) قالَ ذلِكَ ما كُنَّا نَبْغِ ** (64) He said, 'That is what we were seeking.' We seek, because it is the sign (امارة) of the sought thing. Al-Qummi said: That man whom we saw near the rock is the one whom we desire. فَارْتَدَّا عَلى آثارِهِما ** So they retraced their steps. So they returned on the path by which they had come. قَصَصاً ** retracing their steps. They follow a trace (قَصَصاً), meaning: they follow their traces (آثارهما) with a following (اتّباعاً).

(٦٥) فَوَجَدا عَبْداً مِنْ عِبادِنا ** (65) Then they found one of Our servants And he is al-Khidr (peace be upon him), as the narrations (الأخبار) from them (peace be upon them) have widely reported (استفاض).

Al-Qummi reports: He was engaged in prayer, so Moses sat down until he finished the prayer, and then he greeted them both with peace.

And al-Ayyashi narrates from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in the previous narration: Moses (peace be upon him) returned, tracing his steps until he reached him, finding him in the same state, lying down. Moses said to him: "Peace be upon you (السّلام عليك)." He replied: "Peace be upon you (السّلام عليك), O scholar of the Children of Israel." He said: Then he jumped up and took his staff in his hand. Moses said to him: "I have been commanded that أَتَّبِعُكَ عَلى أَنْ تُعَلِّمَنِ مِمَّا عُلِّمْتَ رُشْداً. (66) that I may follow you, so that you may teach me some of the guidance you have been taught.

And in his other narration from both of them (peace be upon them): When they returned, they found that the fish had slipped into the sea. So they traced the steps until they came to their companion on an island among the islands of the sea, either reclining or sitting. Moses (peace be upon him) greeted him with peace (سلّم عليه). He was astonished by the greeting, as he was in a land where there was no greeting of peace (سلام). He asked: "Who are you?" He replied: "I am Moses son of Imran, whom Allah spoke to directly (كلّمه الله تكليماً)." He said: "Yes." He asked: "What is your need?" He replied: "I have come so that you may teach me some of the guidance you have been taught (مِمَّا عُلِّمْتَ رُشْداً)." He said: "I have been entrusted with an affair (أمر) that you cannot endure, and you have been entrusted with an affair that I cannot endure." Then the scholar (العالم) spoke to him about the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) and about the afflictions (البلاء) that would befall them (may the blessings of Allah be upon them), until their weeping intensified. Then he spoke to him about the virtue (فضل) of the Family of Muhammad (peace be upon them), until Moses (peace be upon him) began to say: "O, would that I were one of the Family of Muhammad (peace be upon them)." And he even mentioned so-and-so (فلاناً) and so-and-so (فلاناً), and the sending of the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) to his people, and what he would suffer from them and their denial of Him. And he mentioned to him the interpretation (تأويل) of this Ayah: وَنُقَلِّبُ أَفْئِدَتَهُمْ وَأَبْصارَهُمْ كَما لَمْ يُؤْمِنُوا بِهِ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ (And We will turn their hearts and their sights, just as they did not believe in it the first time) when the covenant (الميثاق) was taken from them.

And al-Qummi narrates from al-Rida (peace be upon him): Moses came to the scholar (العالم) and found him on an island among the islands of the sea, either sitting or reclining—the narration (الحديث) is as al-Ayyashi mentioned it.

And in al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Al-Khidr was a Messenger-Prophet (نبيّاً مرسلاً) whom Allah sent to his people. He called them to His Oneness (توحيده) and to acknowledge His Prophets, His Messengers, and His Books. His sign (آيته) was that he would not sit upon dry wood or white earth except that it would shake and turn green (اهتزّت خضراً). He was only named al-Khidr (الخضر) for that reason. His name was Balya son of Malka son of Amir son of Arfakhshad son of Sam son of Noah. آتَيْناهُ رَحْمَةً مِنْ عِنْدِنا ** (whom We had granted a mercy from Us)—this is the revelation (الوحي) and prophethood (النّبوة)—وَعَلَّمْناهُ مِنْ لَدُنَّا عِلْماً ** (and whom We had taught a knowledge from Our own). It is said: meaning, that knowledge which is specific to Us, which is the knowledge of the unseen (علم الغيوب).

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: He possessed knowledge that was not written for Moses (peace be upon him) on the Tablets (الألواح). And Moses (peace be upon him) thought that all the things he needed were in his Ark (تابوته) and that all knowledge had been written for him on the Tablets.

(٦٦) قالَ لَهُ مُوسى هَلْ أَتَّبِعُكَ عَلى أَنْ تُعَلِّمَنِ مِمَّا عُلِّمْتَ رُشْداً ** (66) Moses said to him, 'May I follow you so that you may teach me some of the guidance you have been taught?' And it was recited with two fathahs (بفتحتين). (٦٧) قالَ إِنَّكَ لَنْ تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِيَ صَبْراً. (67) He said, 'Indeed, you will not be able to have patience with me.' In al-Ilal (العلل), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Al-Khidr said: إِنَّكَ لَنْ تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِيَ صَبْراً ** (Indeed, you will not be able to have patience with me) because I have been entrusted with an affair that you cannot bear, and you have been entrusted with a knowledge that I cannot bear. Moses (peace be upon him) said: "Rather, I shall be able to have patience with you." So Al-Khidr said: "Indeed, analogy (القياس) has no place in the knowledge of Allah and His command."

(٦٨) وَكَيْفَ تَصْبِرُ عَلى ما لَمْ تُحِطْ بِهِ خُبْراً. (68) "And how can you be patient about that of which you have no comprehensive knowledge?" (٦٩) قالَ سَتَجِدُنِي إِنْ شاءَ اللهُ صابِراً وَلا أَعْصِي لَكَ أَمْراً ** (69) He said, "If Allah wills, you will find me patient, and I will not disobey you in any matter." He said: So when he made the exception (الاستثناء) of the Will (المشيّة), He accepted him.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them both), in a narration of his: "And they did not desire our knowledge (علمنا) as Moses desired [to go] to the scholar (العالم) and asked him for companionship (الصّحبة) so that he might learn knowledge (العلم) from him and that he might guide him. So when he asked the scholar for that, the scholar knew that Moses would not be able to accompany him, nor endure his knowledge, nor remain with him. It was then that the scholar said: وَكَيْفَ تَصْبِرُ عَلى ما لَمْ تُحِطْ بِهِ خُبْراً (And how can you be patient about that of which you have no comprehensive knowledge?). So Moses (peace be upon him) said to him, while being humble (خاضع) towards him, seeking to soften him towards himself so that he might accept him: سَتَجِدُنِي إِنْ شاءَ اللهُ (If Allah wills, you will find me...), [up to] the end of the Ayah."

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Moses (peace be upon him) was more knowledgeable than al-Khidr."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "If I were between Moses (peace be upon him) and al-Khidr, I would inform them that I am more knowledgeable than them both, and I would inform them of what is not in their possession, because Moses (peace be upon him) and al-Khidr (peace be upon him) were given the knowledge of what was (علم ما كان), but they were not given the knowledge of what will be (علم ما يكون) and what is coming to pass until the Hour is established. And we have inherited this from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم as an inheritance."

(٧٠) قالَ فَإِنِ اتَّبَعْتَنِي فَلا تَسْئَلْنِي عَنْ شَيْءٍ حَتَّى أُحْدِثَ لَكَ مِنْهُ ذِكْراً. (He said, 'If you follow me, do not question me about anything until I mention it to you.')

The Almighty's saying: **قالَ فَإِنِ اتَّبَعْتَنِي فَلا تَسْئَلْنِي ** (He said, 'If you follow me, do not question me)

And it was read with the heavy نُون عَنْ شَيْءٍ حَتَّى أُحْدِثَ لَكَ مِنْهُ ذِكْراً. (about anything until I mention it to you.)

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He says: **فَلا تَسْئَلْنِي عَنْ شَيْءٍ ** (do not question me about anything) that I do, and do not object to it against me until I Myself inform you of its explanation. He said, "Yes."

(٧١) فَانْطَلَقا along the shore, seeking the ship, حَتَّى إِذا رَكِبا فِي السَّفِينَةِ خَرَقَها al-Khidr (did this). قالَ Moses (said): "Did you pierce it to drown its people?" And it was read with the attribution (بالإسناد) to the people (الى الأهل): لَقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْئاً إِمْراً (meaning) great (عظيماً).

فَانْطَلَقا حَتَّى إِذا رَكِبا فِي السَّفِينَةِ خَرَقَها قالَ أَخَرَقْتَها لِتُغْرِقَ أَهْلَها لَقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْئاً إِمْراً (71) Then they set out until, when they boarded the ship, he pierced it. He said, 'Did you pierce it to drown its people? You have certainly committed a terrible thing.'

Al-Qummi said: It is the reprehensible thing (المنكر). And Moses (peace be upon him) used to reject injustice (الظّلم), so this was the greatest thing he had seen.

(٧٢) قالَ أَلَمْ أَقُلْ إِنَّكَ لَنْ تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِيَ صَبْراً. (72) He said, 'Did I not tell you that you will not be able to bear with me patiently?' (٧٣) قالَ لا تُؤاخِذْنِي بِما نَسِيتُ وَلا تُرْهِقْنِي مِنْ أَمْرِي عُسْراً ** (73) He said, 'Do not take me to task for what I forgot, and do not impose a difficulty on me in my affair.' And do not cover me with difficulty in my affair by way of constraint (المضايقة) and taking me to task (المؤاخذة) for what was forgotten (المنسيّ), for indeed that will make following you difficult for me. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: The first [instance] from Musa (peace be upon him) was forgetfulness (نسياناً).

(٧٤) **فَانْطَلَقا حَتَّى إِذا لَقِيا غُلاماً فَقَتَلَهُ قالَ أَقَتَلْتَ نَفْساً زَكِيَّةً بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍ لَقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْئاً نُكْراً ** (74) Then they set out until, when they met a boy, he killed him. He said, 'Have you killed an innocent soul without [his having killed] any soul? You have certainly committed a terrible thing.')

فَانْطَلَقا ** meaning, after they had departed from the ship. حَتَّى إِذا لَقِيا غُلاماً فَقَتَلَهُ ** without deliberation (تَرَوّ) or investigation of his condition (استكشاف حال). قالَ أَقَتَلْتَ نَفْساً زَكِيَّةً ** pure of sins (طاهرة الذّنُوب). It was recited (قُرِئ) as زاكية. بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍ ** meaning, without him having killed a soul for which he should be executed (فَتُقَاد) (1) in retribution. لَقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْئاً نُكْراً ** meaning, something abominable (مُنْكَراً). It was recited (قُرِئ) with two ḍammahs (بِضَمَّتَيْن).

In al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Moses (peace be upon him) became angry and seized him by his collar (بِتَلْبِيبِهِ) and قالَ أَقَتَلْتَ ** the Ayah. Al-Khidr said: "Indeed, intellects (العُقُول) do not judge the command of Allah; rather, the command of Allah judges them. So submit to what you see from me and be patient with it, for I had already informed you: إِنَّكَ لَنْ تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِيَ صَبْراً (You will not be able to bear patiently with me)."

(٧٥) قالَ أَلَمْ أَقُلْ لَكَ إِنَّكَ لَنْ تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِيَ صَبْراً ** (75) He said, 'Did I not tell you that you will not be able to bear with me patiently?' It was said that He (al-Khidr) added the word لَكَ (to you) in it as a confrontation with reproach for rejecting the injunction, and as a branding with little steadfastness and patience, because of the repeated aversion (الاشمئزاز) and disdain (الاستنكاف) from him (Musa عليه السلام). And he (Musa عليه السلام) did not heed the reminder the first time, until he increased the objection a second time.

(١) القَوَد ـ بالتحريك ـ : القصاص. (1) Al-Qawad (القَوَد)—with the vowelization (بالتحريك)—means retribution (القصاص).

(٧٦) قالَ إِنْ سَأَلْتُكَ عَنْ شَيْءٍ بَعْدَها فَلا تُصاحِبْنِي ** (76) He said, 'If I question you about anything after this, do not keep me company. وان سألت صحبتك And if I question, then do not keep my company. قَدْ بَلَغْتَ مِنْ لَدُنِّي عُذْراً ** You will then have an excuse on my part.' قد وجدت عذراً من قبلي لما خالفتك ثلاث مرّات You have found an excuse from my side because I have opposed you three times. وقرئ بتخفيف النّون وبإسكان الدّال. And it was recited with the lightening (تخفيف) of the nūn and the quiescence (إسكان) of the dāl. روي عن النّبي صلَّى الله عليه وآله : رَحم الله اخي موسى استحيى فقال ذلك لو لَبث مع صاحبه لَابصر اعجب الأعاجيب. It is narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: "May Allah have mercy upon my brother Moses. He felt ashamed, so he said that. Had he remained with his companion, he would have witnessed the most wondrous of wonders (أعجب الأعاجيب)."

(٧٧) فَانْطَلَقا حَتَّى إِذا أَتَيا أَهْلَ قَرْيَةٍ. (77) Then they set out until, when they came to the people of a town, In al-Ilal (العلل) and al-Ayyashi (العيّاشي), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is al-Nasirah (النّاصرة), and the Christians (النّصارى) are named after it. اسْتَطْعَما أَهْلَها فَأَبَوْا أَنْ يُضَيِّفُوهُما فَوَجَدا فِيها جِداراً يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَنْقَضَ ** they asked its people for food, but they refused to offer them hospitality. Then they found in it a wall which was about to collapse. (يَنْكَسِرُ) (it breaks), meaning it is near (يُدَانِي) to fall. The will (الْإِرَادَة) was metaphorically borrowed (اسْتُعِيرَتْ) for the state of being on the verge (لِلْمُشَارَفَة). And in al-Majma': Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) read it as يَنْقَاص (with the unpointed صاد and the alif), and its meaning is splitting (الِانْشِقَاق). فَأَقامَهُ ** So he set it right. By placing his hand upon it. Thus it is in al-Ilal (العلل), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: قالَ لَوْ شِئْتَ لَاتَّخَذْتَ عَلَيْهِ أَجْراً. He said, 'Had you wished, you would have surely taken a wage for it.' Al-Ayyashi (العيّاشي), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Meaning bread (خُبْزاً) that we eat, for we are hungry. And it was read as لَتَخِذْتَ (with a kasrah on the kha, lightened), meaning لَأَخَذْتَ (you would have taken).

(٧٨) قالَ هذا فِراقُ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنِكَ سَأُنَبِّئُكَ بِتَأْوِيلِ ما لَمْ تَسْتَطِعْ عَلَيْهِ صَبْراً (78) He said, 'This is the parting between me and you. I shall inform you about the interpretation of that about which you could not be patient.'

In al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him), as a continuation of the previous narration: The three of them proceeded until they reached the seashore, where a ship was loaded and ready to cross. The owners of the ship said, "We will carry these three people, for they are righteous folk." So they carried them. When the ship sailed into the sea, al-Khidr (peace be upon him) went to the sides of the ship and broke them, filling the holes with rags (الخرق) and clay (الطّين). Moses (peace be upon him) became intensely angry and said to al-Khidr: أَخَرَقْتَها لِتُغْرِقَ أَهْلَها لَقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْئاً إِمْراً (Have you scuttled it to drown its people? You have certainly committed a terrible thing!) Al-Khidr (peace be upon him) said to him: أَلَمْ أَقُلْ إِنَّكَ لَنْ تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِيَ صَبْراً (Did I not say that you will not be able to bear with me patiently?) Moses said: لا تُؤاخِذْنِي بِما نَسِيتُ وَلا تُرْهِقْنِي مِنْ أَمْرِي عُسْراً (Do not take me to task for what I forgot, and do not impose a difficulty on me in my affair.) Then they left the ship. Al-Khidr looked at a boy playing among the children, handsome of face, as if he were a piece of the moon, and in his ears were two pearls (درّتان). Al-Khidr observed him, then took him and killed him. Moses (peace be upon him) leaped upon al-Khidr and threw him to the ground, and said: أَقَتَلْتَ نَفْساً زَكِيَّةً بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍ لَقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْئاً نُكْراً (Have you killed an innocent soul, without [his having killed] another soul? You have certainly committed a horrible thing!) Al-Khidr (peace be upon him) said: أَلَمْ أَقُلْ لَكَ إِنَّكَ لَنْ تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِيَ صَبْراً (Did I not tell you that you will not be able to bear with me patiently?) Moses said: If I question you about anything after this, do not keep me company; you have received an excuse from me. So they proceeded until, in the evening (بالعشيّ), they came to a village called al-Nasirah (النّاصرة), to which the Christians (النّصارى) are attributed. They had never hosted anyone (يضيّفوا أحداً) nor fed a stranger (غريباً) before. They asked them for food, but they neither fed them nor hosted them.

Al-Ayyashi added: And they would not host anyone after them until the Hour (السّاعة) is established. Al-Khidr (peace be upon him) looked at a wall that had shifted and was about to collapse. He placed his hand upon it and said, "Stand up by the permission of Allah," and it stood up. Moses (peace be upon him) said: It was not right for you to set up the wall until they fed us and sheltered us. This is His saying: لَوْ شِئْتَ لَاتَّخَذْتَ عَلَيْهِ أَجْراً (Had you wished, you would surely have taken a wage for it.) Al-Khidr said to him: هذا فِراقُ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنِكَ. (This is the parting between me and you.)

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): It has reached us that Moses (peace be upon him) was patient until He recounted to us some of their story.

(٧٩) أَمَّا السَّفِينَةُ فَكانَتْ لِمَساكِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ فِي الْبَحْرِ فَأَرَدْتُ أَنْ أَعِيبَها ** (79) As for the ship, it belonged to some poor men who worked on the sea, and I intended to damage it, —meaning: I make it possess a defect (ذات عيب)— وَكانَ وَراءَهُمْ مَلِكٌ.** and there was a king behind them.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He used to recite وَراءَهُمْ مَلِكٌ ** (and there was a king behind them), meaning: in front of them (امامهم), who would seize every ship from its owners by force (غَصْباً).

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir and Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both): They both used to recite: "every sound ship (كُلَّ سَفِينَةٍ صالحة) by force (غَصْباً)." He said: And this is the recitation (قراءة) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

And al-Qummi said: Thus it was revealed. He said: And if it was defective (مَعيوبة), he would not take anything from it.

I say: The meaning is constructed upon this (understanding).

(80) وَأَمَّا الْغُلامُ فَكانَ أَبَواهُ مُؤْمِنَيْنِ. (As for the boy, his parents were faithful) In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He used to recite: وَأَمَّا الْغُلامُ فَكانَ كافراً وَأَبَواهُ مُؤْمِنَيْنِ. (As for the boy, he was an unbeliever, and his parents were faithful.) And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from one of the two (Imams) (peace be upon them): He recited: فَكانَ أَبَواهُ مُؤْمِنَيْنِ (his parents were faithful) and he was stamped (طُبِعَ) as an unbeliever (كافراً). And likewise, in al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in al-Qummi: And he was stamped (طُبِعَ) as an unbeliever (كافراً). He said: "Thus it was revealed, and I looked at his forehead and upon it was written: He was stamped (طُبِعَ) as an unbeliever (كافراً)." فَخَشِينا أَنْ يُرْهِقَهُما ** meaning: that he would overwhelm them طُغْياناً وَكُفْراً. (So we feared that he would impose upon them transgression and unfaith) In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah knew that if he remained, he would cause his parents to disbelieve (كَفَرَ أَبَوَاهُ), and they would be tempted by him, and they would go astray through his misguidance. So Allah commanded me to kill him, and by that, He intended to transfer them to the place of His honor (كَرَامَتِهِ) in the Hereafter. And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He feared that if the boy reached maturity, he would invite his parents to disbelief (الكُفْر) and they would respond to him. And narrated from him (peace be upon him): While the knowledgeable man (العَالِم) was walking with Musa (peace be upon him), he came upon a boy who was playing, so he struck him and killed him. Musa said to him: أَقَتَلْتَ نَفْساً ** [the rest of] the Ayah. He said: Then the knowledgeable man (العَالِم) inserted his hand and pulled out his shoulder blade, and behold, upon it was written: "Unbeliever (كَافِر) stamped (مَطْبُوع)." And in a marfu' narration (مَرْفُوعاً): Upon the shoulder blade of the boy whom the knowledgeable man (العَالِم) killed, it was written: "Unbeliever (كَافِر)." And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Najdah al-Haruri wrote to Ibn Abbas asking him about taking the children (الذّرَارِيّ) captive. So he wrote back to him: "As for the children (الذّرَارِيّ), the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم did not kill them. However, al-Khidr (peace be upon him) would kill their unbelievers (كَافِرَهُمْ) and leave their believers (مُؤْمِنَهُمْ). If you know what al-Khidr knows, then kill them."

(٨١) فَأَرَدْنا أَنْ يُبْدِلَهُما رَبُّهُما خَيْراً مِنْهُ ** (So We desired that their Lord should replace him with one better than him) That He would grant them in his stead a child better than him. And يُبَدِّلَهُما (with tashdid on the dal) was recited. زَكاةً ** Purity (طهارة) from sins (الذّنوب) and base morals (الأخلاق الردية). وَأَقْرَبَ رُحْماً ** More merciful (رحمة) and affectionate (عطفاً) towards his parents. And it was recited with two dammahs (رُحُماً). في الكافي والفقيه والمجمع عن الصّادق عليه السّلام والعيّاشي عن أحدهما عليهما السلام : انّهما ابدِلا بالغلام المقتول ابنة فولد منها سبعون نبيّاً. In al-Kafi, al-Faqih, and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in al-Ayyashi, narrated from one of them (peace be upon them both): They were replaced for the slain boy with a daughter, and from her were born seventy prophets.

وَأَمَّا الْجِدارُ فَكانَ لِغُلامَيْنِ يَتِيمَيْنِ فِي الْمَدِينَةِ وَكانَ تَحْتَهُ كَنْزٌ لَهُما وَكانَ أَبُوهُما صالِحاً فَأَرادَ رَبُّكَ أَنْ يَبْلُغا أَشُدَّهُما ** (82) As for the wall, it belonged to two orphan boys in the city, and beneath it was a treasure belonging to them. Their father had been a righteous man. So your Lord desired that they should come of age meaning puberty (الحلم) and maturity of judgment (كمال الرّأي). وَيَسْتَخْرِجا كَنزَهُما رَحْمَةً مِنْ رَبِّكَ.** (and extract their treasure, as a mercy from your Lord.)

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this treasure, and He said: "Indeed, it was neither gold nor silver. Rather, it was four phrases: 'There is no god except I (لا إِله الّا انا). Whoever is certain of death, his teeth shall not laugh (لَمْ يَضْحَكْ سِنّهُ). Whoever is certain of the reckoning (الْحِسابِ), his heart shall not rejoice. And whoever is certain of Divine Decree (الْقَدَرِ), he shall fear none but Allah.'"

And in it (al-Ayyashi), narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): It contained: بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ ** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful). "I wonder at him who is certain of death, how can he rejoice? And I wonder at him who is certain of Divine Decree (القَدَرِ), how can he grieve? And I wonder at him who sees the world (الدْنيا) and its turning away from its people, how can he rely upon it? And it is incumbent upon him who understands from Allah that he should not accuse Allah in His decree (قَضائِهِ) nor deem Him slow in His provision (رِزقهِ)."

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That treasure was a tablet of gold upon which was written: "In the Name of Allah (بِسمِ الله). There is no god except Allah (لا إِله إلّا الله). Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah (مُحمَّدٌ رَسُولُ الله). I wonder at him who knows that death is true (حقٌّ), how can he rejoice? I wonder at him who believes in Divine Decree (القَدَر), how can he grieve? I wonder at him who remembers the Fire (النّار), how can he laugh? I wonder at him who sees the world (الدُّنيا) and the changing state of its people, state after state, how can he be content with it?" And in al-Kanz: there are other narrations with additions and omissions.

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah preserves the child of the believer for up to a thousand years, and there were seven hundred years between the two boys and their parents."

And from Him (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah sets right, through the righteousness (صلاح) of the believing man, his child and his child's child, and preserves him in his small dwelling (دويرته) and the small dwellings around him. They remain continuously under Allah's protection due to his honor (كرامته) before Allah." Then He mentioned the two boys and said: "Do you not see that Allah acknowledged the righteousness of their parents for their sake?"

And in al-Awali, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): "When the knowledgeable man (العالم) erected the wall, Allah revealed to Moses (peace be upon him): 'I shall reward the children according to the striving (سعي) of the fathers: if it is good, then good; and if it is evil, then evil. Do not commit adultery (تزنوا), lest your women commit adultery. Whoever treads upon the bed of a Muslim, his bed shall be trodden upon. As you judge, so shall you be judged (كما تدين تدان).'"

وَما فَعَلْتُهُ ** (And I did not do it) meaning: I did not do what you saw عَنْ أَمْرِي ** (by my own command) meaning: by my own opinion (رأيي). Rather, I did it by the command of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic.

In al-Illal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Regarding His saying: فَأَرَدْتُ أَنْ أَعِيبَها ** (So I intended to damage it), He attributed the intention (الإرادة) in this action to himself due to the reason of mentioning the damaging (التعييب), because he intended to damage it in the sight of the king when he saw it, so that he would not seize it unjustly from the poor people, even though Allah desired their rectification through what He commanded concerning that. And He said regarding His saying: فَخَشِينا أَنْ يُرْهِقَهُما ** (So we feared that they would overwhelm them), He only shared (اشترك) in the self-attribution (الانانيّة) because he feared, and Allah does not fear, because nothing escapes Him and no matter He wills is impossible for Him. Rather, al-Khidr feared that he would be prevented from carrying out what he was commanded, and thus would not attain the reward for executing it. And it occurred to him that Allah had made him a means for the mercy of the boys' parents, so he acted in the middle of the matter with a human element (البشرية), similar to what he had done with Moses (peace be upon him), because at that moment he became the informer (مخبراً), and Moses, the Speaker with Allah (كليم الله), became the informed (مخبراً). This was not due to al-Khidr deserving a higher rank than Moses (peace be upon him)—for Moses is superior to al-Khidr—but rather it was due to Moses deserving the clarification (التّبيين). And He said regarding His saying: فَأَرادَ رَبُّكَ (So your Lord desired), He absolved himself of self-attribution (الانانيّة) at the end of the story and attributed the entire intention (الإرادة) to Allah, exalted be His remembrance, in that instance, because nothing remained of what he had done to be informed about afterwards, and Moses would become the informed, listening attentively to his speech and following him. Thus, he stripped himself of self-attribution (الانانيّة) and intention (الإرادة) with the stripping of the sincere servant. Then he became one who disavowed (متنصّلاً) (1) what had come from him regarding the attribution of self-attribution (الانانيّة) in the first part of the story and the claim of partnership (الأشتراك) in the second part of the story, so he said: رَحْمَةً مِنْ رَبِّكَ وَما فَعَلْتُهُ عَنْ أَمْرِي ذلِكَ تَأْوِيلُ ما لَمْ تَسْطِعْ عَلَيْهِ صَبْراً ** (as a mercy from your Lord. And I did not do it by my own command. This is the interpretation of what you could not bear patiently), meaning: ما لم تستطع (what you could not bear), and the letter ت (tā’) was omitted for lightness (تخفيفاً).

It is said that among the benefits (فوائد) of this story is that a person should not be conceited (يعجب) by his knowledge, nor should he rush to deny what he does not approve of, for perhaps there is a secret (سرّاً) in it that he does not know. And that he should persist in learning, humble himself before the teacher (المعلّم), and observe proper conduct (الأدب) in speech. And that he should warn the offender (المجرم) about his offense and pardon him until his persistence (إصراره) is confirmed, and then he should abandon him (يهاجر عنه).

(83) وَيَسْئَلُونَكَ عَنْ ذِي الْقَرْنَيْنِ قُلْ سَأَتْلُوا عَلَيْكُمْ مِنْهُ ذِكْراً. And they ask you about Dhul Qarnayn. Say, 'I will recite to you a mention of him.'

In Qurb al-Isnad, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): A group of Jews came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said to Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him): "My grandfather, ask permission for us to see your cousin, so that we may question him." He (Abu al-Hasan) said: So I went in to Ali (peace be upon him) and informed him. He (Ali) said: "What do you want from me? For I am a servant among the servants of Allah, and I know nothing except what my Lord has taught me." Then he said: "Grant them permission." So they entered. He (Ali) said: "Will you ask me about what you came for, or shall I inform you?" They said: "Inform us." He said: "You have come to ask me about Dhul Qarnayn." They said: "Yes." He said: "He was a youth from the people of Rome (الرّوم), then he became a king, and he reached the rising place of the sun and its setting place, and then he built the barrier (السدّ) there." They said: "We bear witness that this is such and such."

And in al-Qummi: When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم informed them of the story of Moses (peace be upon him), his young companion, and al-Khidr, they said: "Then inform us about a traveler (طائف) who traversed the East and the West—who is he and what is his story?" So Allah revealed [this Ayah].

And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He was asked about Dhul Qarnayn, whether he was a prophet (نبيّاً) or a king (ملكاً). He replied: "Neither a prophet nor a king, but rather a servant who loved Allah, so Allah loved him. He was sincere to Allah, so He was sincere to him. He sent him to his people, and they struck him on his right horn (قرنه الأيمن), so he disappeared from them for as long as Allah willed him to disappear. Then He sent him a second time, and they struck him on his left horn (قرنه الأيسر), so he disappeared from them for as long as Allah willed. Then He sent him a third time, and Allah established him firmly in the earth. And among you is one like him," meaning himself (Ali).

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah sent Dhul Qarnayn to his people, and he was struck on his right horn, so Allah caused him to die for five hundred years. Then Allah sent him to them after that, and he was struck on his left horn, so Allah caused him to die for five hundred years. Then Allah sent him to them after that, and He made him sovereign over the Easts of the earth and its Wests, from where the sun rises to where it sets. And that is His saying: **حَتَّى إِذا بَلَغَ مَغْرِبَ الشَّمْسِ ** (until, when he reached the setting place of the sun) [the rest of] the Ayah.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Dhul Qarnayn was neither a prophet (نبيّاً) nor a messenger (رسولاً). He was a servant who loved Allah, so He loved him. He was sincere to Allah, so He was sincere to him. He called his people, and they struck him on one of his horns and killed him. Then Allah resurrected him, and they struck him on his other horn and killed him.

And in another narration, he (peace be upon him) was asked about him: Was he a king or a prophet? And regarding his two horns: Were they gold or silver? He replied: "He was neither a prophet nor a king, and his two horns were neither gold nor silver, but the account is as mentioned."

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Dhul Qarnayn was not a prophet, but he was a righteous servant who loved Allah, so He loved him. He was sincere to Allah, so Allah was sincere to him. He was only named Dhul Qarnayn (The Possessor of the Two Horns) because he called his people, and they struck him on his horn, so he disappeared from them for a time. Then he returned to them, and he was struck on his other horn. And among you is one like him.

And in al-Ayyashi: [A narration] close to it. And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Allah did not send forth prophet-kings (أنبياء ملوكاً) on earth after Noah except four: the first of them is Dhul Qarnayn, whose name is Ayyash; and David; and Solomon; and Joseph. As for Ayyash, he ruled what is between the East and the West. As for David, he ruled what is between the lands of al-Shamat (الشامات) up to the lands of Istakhr (إصطخر). Likewise was the kingdom of Solomon (peace be upon him). As for Joseph, he ruled Egypt (مصر) and its deserts (براريها), and did not cross beyond it to other lands.

And in al-Khisal, narrated marfu'an (مرفوعاً): Four ruled the entire earth: two believers and two disbelievers. As for the two believers, they are Solomon son of David and Dhul Qarnayn. As for the two disbelievers, they are Nimrod (نمرود) and Nebuchadnezzar (بخت النّصر). The name of Dhul Qarnayn is Abdullah ibn al-Dahhak (عبد الله بن ضحّاك).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He was asked about Dhul Qarnayn, and he said: "He was a righteous servant, and his name was Ayyash. Allah chose him and sent him to a generation (قرن) of the early generations in the region of the West, and that was after the Flood of Noah (peace be upon him). They struck him on the horn of his right head, and he died from it. Then Allah resurrected him after one hundred years. Then He sent him to a generation of the early generations in the region of the East, and they denied him and struck him a blow on the horn of his left head, and he died from it. Then Allah resurrected him after one hundred years and compensated him for the two blows on his head with two hollow horns (قرنين أجوفين) in the place of the blows. And He placed the might of his kingdom and the sign of his prophethood (آية نبوّته) in his two horns. Then Allah raised him to the lowest heaven (السماء الدنيا) and uncovered for him the entire earth—its mountains, its plains, and its valleys—until he saw what was between the East and the West. And Allah gave him of every thing, by which he recognized the truth and the falsehood. And He supported him in his two horns with a piece (كسف) from the sky containing darkness, thunder, and lightning. Then He was sent down to the earth, and He revealed to him concerning the western and eastern regions of the earth: 'The lands have been folded up for you, and the servants have been subdued for you, so you have instilled fear of yourself in them.' So he traveled to the region of the West. When he passed by a village, he would roar in it like an angry lion. He sent forth from his horn darkness containing thunder, lightning, and thunderbolts that destroyed whoever opposed him and disobeyed him. He did not reach the setting place of the sun until the people of the East and the West submitted to him." He said: "And that is the saying of Allah: **إِنَّا مَكَّنَّا لَهُ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (Indeed, We established him in the land) [the rest of] the Ayah.

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Dhul Qarnayn was given a choice between the difficult cloud (السّحاب الصّعب) and the docile cloud (السحاب الذّلول), so he chose the docile one. He rode the docile one, and when he reached a people, he would be his own messenger to them, so that the messengers would not be denied.

And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He was asked about Dhul Qarnayn, and he said: "The clouds were subjugated for him, the means (الأسباب) were brought near for him, and the light (النّور) was extended for him." He was asked: "How was the light extended for him?" He replied: "He would illuminate the night just as he illuminates the day."

And in al-Ikmal and al-Khara'ij, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about Dhul Qarnayn, how he was able to reach the East and the West. He replied: "Allah subjugated the clouds for him, the means were made easy for him, and the light was extended for him, and the night and the day were the same for him." And it is added in al-Khara'ij: "And he saw in a dream as if he drew near to the sun until he grasped its horn (قرنها) in its East and its West. When he recounted his vision to his people and informed them, they named him Dhul Qarnayn. So he called them to Allah, and they submitted." [The rest of] the narration.

(٨٤) إِنَّا مَكَّنَّا لَهُ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَآتَيْناهُ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ ** (84) Indeed We had established him in the land, and given him the means of everything. [We gave him] what he desired and turned toward, [which was] a means (سَبَباً). It is said: a connection (وصلة) that would lead him to it, consisting of knowledge (العلم), power (القدرة), and tools (الآلة). And al-Qummi narrates from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): meaning a guide (دليلا).

(٨٥) فَأَتْبَعَ سَبَباً ** (85) So he followed a course. Meaning, he desired to reach the West (المَغرب), so he followed a means (سَبَباً) that would lead him to it. And it was read (وقرئ) with the hamzah cut off (بقطع الهمزة) and the tā’ (التّاء) lightened.

(٨٦) حَتَّى إِذا بَلَغَ مَغْرِبَ الشَّمْسِ وَجَدَها تَغْرُبُ فِي عَيْنٍ حَمِئَةٍ ** (86) When he reached the setting place of the sun, he found it setting in a muddy spring, [A spring] possessing ḥama’ah (حماة), which is black mud (الطين الأسود). And it was read (وقرئ) ḥāmiyah (حامية), meaning hot (حارّة). And it is possible that it combines both descriptions. It is said that perhaps he reached the shore of the surrounding ocean (البحر المحيط) and saw it thus, since there was nothing within the range of his sight except water. For this reason, He said: وَجَدَها تَغْرُبُ ** (he found it setting), and He did not say: it was setting (كانت تغرب). And al-Ayyashi narrates from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): فِي عَيْنٍ حَمِئَةٍ ** (in a muddy spring) [means] in a sea below the city which is near the West (المغرب), meaning Jābalqā (جابلقا). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): When he reached the hot spring (العين الحامية) along with the sun, he found it setting therein, and with it were seventy thousand angels dragging it with iron chains (سلاسل الحديد) and hooks (الكلاليب). They drag it from the bottom of the sea in the right quarter (قطر) of the earth, just as a ship runs upon the surface of the water. وَوَجَدَ عِنْدَها ** (and found near it) near that spring (العين) قَوْماً ** (a people), [meaning] disbelieving people (ناسا كفرة). قُلْنا يا ذَا الْقَرْنَيْنِ إِمَّا أَنْ تُعَذِّبَ (We said, 'O Dhul Qarnayn! You may either punish them'), meaning by killing them due to their disbelief (كفرهم), وَإِمَّا أَنْ تَتَّخِذَ فِيهِمْ حُسْناً ** (or treat them kindly), by guiding them (بإرشادهم) and teaching them the Divine Laws (الشرايع).

قالَ أَمَّا مَنْ ظَلَمَ (٨٧) (87) He said: 'As for him who is an evildoer, meaning, invite them to faith first. So as for him whom you invited, and he wronged himself by persisting in his disbelief (كفره), فَسَوْفَ نُعَذِّبُهُ ** then We will punish him with the punishment of this world. ثُمَّ يُرَدُّ إِلى رَبِّهِ ** then he will be returned to his Lord in his place of return, فَيُعَذِّبُهُ عَذاباً نُكْراً ** and He will punish him with a terrible punishment—a punishment (عذاباً منكراً) the like of which has not been known in the Hereafter. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): meaning, in the Fire (النّار).

(٨٨) وَأَمَّا مَنْ آمَنَ وَعَمِلَ صالِحاً فَلَهُ جَزاءً الْحُسْنى ** (88) But as for him who believes and acts righteously, for him shall be the best reward. The recompense for his good deed (الحُسنى). And it was recited as جزاءً (with tanwin and in the accusative case (منوّناً منصوباً)), meaning: for him shall be the best reward (المثوبة الحسنى) as a recompense. وَسَنَقُولُ لَهُ مِنْ أَمْرِنا ** and We shall speak to him of Our command from what We command him with, such as the land tax (الخراج) and other things, with ease (يُسْراً), meaning: easy, facilitated, and not burdensome (غير شاقّ).

(٨٩) ثُمَّ أَتْبَعَ سَبَباً ** (89) Then he followed a course Then he followed a path (طريقاً) that led him to the East. (٩٠) حَتَّى إِذا بَلَغَ مَطْلِعَ الشَّمْسِ ** (90) until, when he reached the rising place of the sun, It is said that this means the place upon which the sun first rises from the inhabited (معمورة) part of the earth. وَجَدَها تَطْلُعُ عَلى قَوْمٍ لَمْ نَجْعَلْ لَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِها سِتْراً He found it rising on a people for whom We had not provided any shelter from it. In al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They did not know the craft of making houses (البيوت). And al-Qummi said: They did not know the craft of making clothes (الثياب). And al-Ayyashi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He came upon a people whom the sun had burned and whose bodies and colors it had changed until it rendered them like darkness (كالظّلمة).

(91) كَذلِكَ ** meaning, his affair is as We described it regarding the loftiness of the place and the vastness of the dominion. His commands therein are like His command among the people of the West (أهل المغرب). وَقَدْ أَحَطْنا بِما لَدَيْهِ خُبْراً ** (And We have encompassed whatever is with him in knowledge) of armies, signs (الآيات), numbers, and means (الأسباب). For indeed, despite their multitude, nothing encompasses them except the knowledge of the Subtle, the All-Aware (اللّطيف الخبير).

(٩٢) ثُمَّ أَتْبَعَ سَبَباً ** (92) Then he followed a course meaning a third path (طريقاً ثالثاً) lying across (مُعْتَرِضاً) between the East and the West, extending from the South to the North. And al-Ayyashi narrates from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): a course (is) in the direction (ناحية) of darkness (الظلمة). (٩٣) حَتَّى إِذا بَلَغَ بَيْنَ السَّدَّيْنِ ** (93) until, when he reached between the two barriers, between the two mountains (الجبلين) between which his barrier (سدّه) was built. And it was read with the damma on the sin (بضم السين) [as السُّدَّيْنِ]. وَجَدَ مِنْ دُونِهِما قَوْماً لا يَكادُونَ يَفْقَهُونَ قَوْلاً ** he found a people who could scarcely understand any speech due to the strangeness (غرابة) of their language (لغتهم) and the scarcity (قلّة) of their intelligence (فطنتهم). And it was read with the damma on the ya (بضم الياء) and the kasra on the qaf (وكسر القاف) [as يُفْقِهونَ], meaning: they do not make the listener understand their speech (كلامهم), nor do they articulate it clearly (يبينونه) due to their stammering (تَلَعْثُمِهِمْ) in it.

(٩٤) قالُوا يا ذَا الْقَرْنَيْنِ إِنَّ يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ ** (94) They said, 'O Dhul Qarnayn! Indeed, Gog and Magog And it was recited with a hamzah (بالهمزة). It is said that they are two tribes from the progeny of Yafith son of Nuh (Noah). And it is said that Gog (يَأْجُوجَ) is from the Turks (الترك) and Magog (وَمَأْجُوجَ) is from the mountain (الجبل).

In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Hadi (peace be upon him): All of the Turks (الترك) and the Slavs (السّقالب) and Gog and Magog (يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ) [and al-Sayr (الصين - China, variant reading)] are from Yafith, wherever they may be.

مُفْسِدُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** are doing mischief on the earth.' Meaning: in our land, through killing, destruction (التّخريب), and the ruin of crops (إتلاف الزّروع).

And al-Ayyashi narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): They said, 'O Dhul Qarnayn! Indeed, Gog and Magog are behind these two mountains, and they cause mischief on the earth. When it is the season (ابان) for our crops (زروعنا) and our fruits (ثمارنا), they emerge against us from these two barriers (السّدين), and they graze (فرعوا) upon our fruits and our crops until they leave nothing of them.

فَهَلْ نَجْعَلُ لَكَ خَرْجاً (١) So shall we pay you a tribute (1) He said: meaning, wealth (مالاً) that we pay to you every year. And it was recited kharājan (خراجاً).

عَلى أَنْ تَجْعَلَ بَيْنَنا وَبَيْنَهُمْ سَدًّا ** on condition that you build a barrier between us and them?' which prevents their emergence against us. And it was recited with the dammah (بضمّ السين) on the sīn (i.e., suddan).

(٩٥) قالَ ما مَكَّنِّي فِيهِ رَبِّي خَيْرٌ ** (95) He said, 'What my Lord has established me in is better [than what you offer].' What He has made me established in, of wealth (المال) and dominion (الملك), is better than what you offer me of tribute (الخراج), and I have no need for it. And it was read as مكّنني with two nūns. فَأَعِينُونِي بِقُوَّةٍ 'So help me with strength' [Help me] with workers (فعلة) or with what I can strengthen myself with, of tools (الآلات). أَجْعَلْ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُمْ رَدْماً ** 'I will erect a barrier between you and them.' A strong barrier (حاجزاً حصيناً), and it is larger than a dam (السّد).

(٩٦) آتُونِي زُبَرَ الْحَدِيدِ ** (Bring me ingots of iron!) Its pieces (قِطَعُهُ). And الزُّبَر (ingots) is the large piece (الْقِطْعَة الْكَبِيرَة). It was said that this does not contradict the rejection of the tribute (الْخَرَاج) and the restriction to assistance (الْمَعُونَة), because الْإِيتَاء (bringing/giving) here means handing over (الْمُنَاوَلَة). And it was read (قُرِئَ) as ائْتُونِي (bring to me) with a kasrah on the hamzah (بِكَسْرِ الْهَمْزَة), meaning: "Come to me with it (جِيئُونِي بِهَا)," with the deletion of the yā' (بِحَذْفِ الْيَاء). حَتَّى إِذا ساوى بَيْنَ الصَّدَفَيْنِ ** (When he had leveled up [the gap] between the two mountain-sides,) Between the two sides of the mountains (بَيْنَ جَانِبَيِ الْجَبَلَيْن) by stacking them (بِتَنْضِيدِهَا). And it was read (قُرِئَ) with two ḍammahs (بِضَمَّتَيْن) [i.e., الصُّدُفَيْن] and with a ḍammah on the ḍād and a sukūn on the dāl [i.e., الصُّدْفَيْن]. قالَ انْفُخُوا ** (he said, 'Blow!') Meaning, he said to the workers (لِلْعَمَلَة): "Blow into the bellows (فِي الْأَكْوَار)." حَتَّى إِذا جَعَلَهُ ناراً ** (When he had made it a fire,) Like fire (كَالنَّار) through heating (بِالْإِحْمَاء). قالَ آتُونِي أُفْرِغْ عَلَيْهِ قِطْراً ** (he said, 'Bring me molten copper to pour over it.') Meaning, bring me قِطْرًا (molten copper) which I may pour over it, meaning copper (نُحَاسًا). And it was read (قُرِئَ) as ائْتُونِي.

Al-Qummi (narrated): So he commanded them to bring him the iron, and he placed it between the two mountain-sides (الصَّدَفَيْن), meaning between the two mountains (بَيْنَ الْجَبَلَيْن), until he leveled between them. Then he commanded them to bring the fire, so they brought it, and they blew beneath the iron until the iron became like fire. Then he poured the molten copper (الْقِطْر) over it, which is brass (الصُّفْر), until he sealed it.

And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), in a narration: So Dhul-Qarnayn made a gate between them of copper (نُحَاس), iron (حَدِيد), pitch (زِفْت), and tar (قِطْرَان), thus preventing them from exiting.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): So they dug up a mountain of iron for him, and they extracted pieces for him like bricks (اللَّبِن). He threw some of it upon others between the two mountain-sides (الصَّدَفَيْن). And Dhul-Qarnayn was the first person to build a barrier (رَدْمًا) upon the face of the earth. Then he placed firewood (الْحَطَب) upon it and ignited the fire therein, and he placed bellows (مَنَافِيخ) upon it, and they blew upon it. He said: When it melted, he said: "Bring me molten copper (قِطْر)." So they dug up a mountain of brass (مَسّ) for him, and they threw it upon the iron, and it melted with it and mixed with it.

(97) فَمَا اسْطاعُوا ** meaning: So they were not able (فما استطاعا), and the tāʾ (التاء) was omitted. He said: meaning Gog and Magog (يأجوج ومأجوج) أَنْ يَظْهَرُوهُ ** to ascend above it by climbing due to its height and smoothness, **وَمَا اسْتَطاعُوا لَهُ نَقْباً ** nor were they able to pierce it, due to its thickness and hardness.

(98) قالَ هذا ** He said: This dam (السّد) or the ability to construct it رَحْمَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّي ** is a mercy from my Lord upon His servants. فَإِذا جاءَ وَعْدُ رَبِّي ** But when the promise of my Lord comes (with the establishment of the Hour (السّاعة)), جَعَلَهُ دَكَّاءَ ** He will turn it into dust (مدكوكاً), flattened, leveled with the ground. And it was read (دكّاء) with madd (بالمدّ), meaning a level ground (أرضاً مستوية). **وَكانَ وَعْدُ رَبِّي حَقًّا ** And the promise of my Lord is true, inevitably coming to pass.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: When it is before the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) in the Last Time (آخر الزّمان), that dam will collapse, and Gog and Magog (يأجوج ومأجوج) will emerge into the world and consume the people. This is His saying: حَتَّى إِذا فُتِحَتْ يَأْجُوجُ وَمَأْجُوجُ وَهُمْ مِنْ كُلِّ حَدَبٍ يَنْسِلُونَ. (until when Gog and Magog are let loose, and they swarm down from every elevation.)

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): There is no man among them who dies until a thousand male children are born to him from his loins. Then he said: They are the most numerous creatures created after the angels (الملائكة).

And in al-Khisal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The world consists of seven climes (أقاليم): Gog and Magog (يأجوج ومأجوج), the Romans (الروم), the Chinese (الصين), the Zanj (الزنج), the people of Moses (peace be upon him), and the clime of Babylon (بابل). And narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): He counted the emergence of Gog and Magog (يأجوج ومأجوج) among the signs (الآيات) that will occur before the Hour (الساعة).

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): He was asked about Gog and Magog (يأجوج ومأجوج). He said: "Gog (يأجوج) is a nation (أمّة) and Magog (مأجوج) is a nation, and every nation consists of four hundred nations. No man among them dies until he sees a thousand males from his loins, all of whom have borne arms." It was said: "O Messenger of Allah, describe them to us." He said: "They are of three types (أصناف). One type among them is like the Arz (الأرز)." It was asked: "O Messenger of Allah, what is the Arz (الأرز)?" He said: "A tall tree in al-Sham (الشام)." "And a type among them whose height and width are equal, and these are those against whom neither mountain nor iron can stand. And a type among them, one of them spreads one of his ears as a mattress and covers himself with the other. They do not pass by an elephant, nor a wild beast, nor a camel, nor a pig, but they consume it. And whoever dies among them, they eat him. Their vanguard is in al-Sham (الشّام) and their rear guard is in Khurasan (خراسان). They drink the rivers of the East (المشرق) and the Sea of Tiberias (بحيرة طبريّة)."

And therein (in al-Majma') and it came in the Hadith (الحديث): They toil diligently in their digging throughout their day until, when evening comes and they are about to see the rays of the sun, they say: "We shall return tomorrow and open it," and they do not make an exception (i.e., they do not say إن شاء الله). So they return the next day, and it has become level as it was. Until, when the promise of Allah comes, they say: "Tomorrow we shall open it and emerge, if Allah wills (إن شاء الله)." So they return to it, and it is in the same state as when they left it the day before. They dig it and emerge upon the people. They drink and drain the waters, and the people fortify themselves in their strongholds against them. They shoot their arrows toward the sky, and they return with something resembling blood upon them. They say: "We have conquered the people of the earth and overcome the people of the heaven." Then Allah sends upon them worms (بققا) in the nape of their necks (اقفائهم) which enter their ears, and they perish by them.

The Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) said: "By Him in Whose Hand is the soul of Muhammad, the beasts of the earth will surely grow fat and become intoxicated (تسمن وتسكر) from their flesh, a true intoxication (سكراً)."

And in al-Amali, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about Gog and Magog (يأجوج ومأجوج). He said: "The people diligently chip away with their pickaxes (معاولهم). When night falls, they say: 'Tomorrow we shall finish.' They wake up, and it is stronger than it was the day before. This continues until a man among them submits (to Islam) when Allah wills to fulfill His command. The believer says: 'Tomorrow we shall open it, if Allah wills (إن شاء الله).' They wake up, and then they go to it, and Allah opens it. By Him in Whose Hand is my soul, a man among them will pass by the bank of the valley in Kufan (كوفان), and they will have drunk it until they drained it." It was asked: "O Messenger of Allah, when will this be?" He said: "When nothing remains of the world except like the dregs (صبابة) of a vessel."

And al-Ayyashi (العيّاشي) narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: أَجْعَلْ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُمْ رَدْماً ** (I will set up a barrier between you and them.) He said: "It is al-Taqiyyah (التقيّة)." فَمَا اسْطاعُوا أَنْ يَظْهَرُوهُ وَمَا اسْتَطاعُوا لَهُ نَقْباً ** (So they were not able to ascend above it, nor were they able to pierce it.) He said: "If you practice al-Taqiyyah (التقيّة), they will not be able to devise a trick against you. It is the strong fortress (الحصن الحصين), and it becomes a dam between you and the enemies of Allah, which they cannot pierce." فَإِذا جاءَ وَعْدُ رَبِّي جَعَلَهُ دَكَّاءَ ** (But when the promise of my Lord comes, He will turn it into dust.) He said: "The lifting of al-Taqiyyah (التقيّة) at the time of the unveiling (الكشف), whereby vengeance is taken upon the enemies of Allah."

(٩٩) وَتَرَكْنا بَعْضَهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَمُوجُ فِي بَعْضٍ ** (99) And on that day We shall leave some of them surging upon the others, They mix, crowding, bewildered. And al-Ayyashi narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): meaning the Day of Resurrection. وَنُفِخَ فِي الصُّورِ ** and the Trumpet will be blown, for the commencement of the Hour. **فَجَمَعْناهُمْ جَمْعاً ** and We shall gather them all together. for the reckoning and the recompense.

(١٠٠) **وَعَرَضْنا جَهَنَّمَ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْكافِرِينَ عَرْضاً ** (100) And on that day We shall expose hell to the faithless, a full exposure, And We brought it forth for them, so they witnessed it.

(١٠١) الَّذِينَ كانَتْ أَعْيُنُهُمْ فِي غِطاءٍ عَنْ ذِكْرِي ** (101) those whose eyes were under a cover against My remembrance, concerning My signs (آياتي) and reflecting upon them, وَكانُوا لا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ سَمْعاً ** meaning: and they were deaf to it.

Al-Qummi (said): They would not look at what Allah created of the signs (الآيات), such as the heavens and the earth.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked: "Is the soul capable of knowledge (المعرفة)?" He said: "No." It was said: "Allah says: الَّذِينَ كانَتْ أَعْيُنُهُمْ فِي غِطاءٍ ** (those whose eyes were under a cover) [the rest of] the Ayah." He said: "It is like His saying: وَما كانُوا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ السَّمْعَ وَما كانُوا يُبْصِرُونَ ** (and they could not hear, nor could they see)." It was asked: "Did He then blame them?" He said: "He did not blame them for what He did to them, but He blamed them for what they did. If they had not imposed the burden (upon themselves), there would have been nothing upon them."

And in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): The covering of the eye does not prevent remembrance (الذكر), and remembrance (الذكر) is not seen by the eye. However, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, likened those who disbelieved in the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) to the blind, because they found the saying of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم concerning him burdensome, and لا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ ** (they could not) سَمْعاً.** (hear) it.

And al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah. He said: "By al-Dhikr (الذكر), He means the Wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him)." He said: "They were unable, when Ali (peace be upon him) was mentioned in their presence, to hear his mention, due to the intensity of their hatred and enmity towards him and towards his Household (أهل بيته)."

(١٠٢) **أَفَحَسِبَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَنْ يَتَّخِذُوا عِبادِي مِنْ دُونِي أَوْلِياءَ ** (102) Do the faithless suppose that they can take My servants as guardians instead of Me?

أَفَحَسِبَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (Do the faithless suppose) means: Did they think (أفظنّوا)? And the interrogative (الاستفهام) is for denial (للإِنكار). أَنْ يَتَّخِذُوا عِبادِي مِنْ دُونِي أَوْلِياءَ ** (that they can take My servants as guardians instead of Me?) It is said that this means their taking the angels and the Messiah as deities (معبودين) who would save them from My punishment. Thus, the second object (المفعول الثاني) was omitted due to the contextual indicator (للقرينة).

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He recited أَفَحَسْبُ (a fa ḥasbu) with a ḍammah on the bā' (برفع الباء) and a sukūn on the sīn (وسكون السّين). Its meaning, therefore, is a lie (إفكا) concerning their salvation.

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: It refers to the two of them (يعنيهما) and their followers (أشياعهما) who took them as guardians (أولياء) besides Allah. They intended that by loving the two of them, the two would save them from the punishment of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), yet they were disbelievers (كافرين) through their love for the two of them.

**إِنَّا أَعْتَدْنا جَهَنَّمَ لِلْكافِرِينَ نُزُلاً ** We have indeed prepared hell as a hospitality for the faithless.

He said: [It means] an abode (مأوىً) and a hospitality (نزلاً). Thus, it is prepared by Allah the Almighty for the two of them and for their followers (أشياعهما).

(١٠٣) قُلْ هَلْ نُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِالْأَخْسَرِينَ أَعْمالاً (103) Say, 'Shall We inform you of the biggest losers in action?' (١٠٤) الَّذِينَ ضَلَّ سَعْيُهُمْ فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ** (104) 'Those whose endeavour has been lost in the life of the world,' It was wasted (ضاع) and nullified (بطل) due to their disbelief (لكفرهم). وَهُمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ يُحْسِنُونَ صُنْعاً ** 'while they suppose that they are doing good.' Due to their self-admiration (لعجبهم) and their belief (واعتقادهم) that they are upon the truth (انّهم على الحقّ).

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: It was revealed concerning the Jews (اليهود) and applies to the Khawarij (الخوارج). And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They are the Christians (النّصارى), the priests (القسّيسون), the monks (الرّهبان), the people of doubts (أهل الشبهات) and desires (الاهواء) from the People of the Qiblah (أهل القبلة), the Haruriyyah (الحرورية), and the people of innovation (أهل البدع).

In al-Ihtijaj (الاحتجاج), narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and he said: "The disbelievers (كفرة) among the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب), the Jews (اليهود) and the Christians (النصارى). They were upon the truth, but then they introduced innovations (ابتدعوا) into their religions. وَهُمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ يُحْسِنُونَ صُنْعاً ** (while they suppose that they are doing good.)" Then he said: "And the people of Nahrawan (أهل النهروان) are not far removed from them."

And al-Ayyashi (العيّاشي) narrated the like of it from him (peace be upon him).

And in al-Jawami' (الجوامع), narrated the like of it from him (peace be upon him). It is like His saying: عامِلَةٌ ناصِبَةٌ ** (Toiling, weary,) And he said: "Among them are the people of Harura' (اهل حروراء)."

(١٠٥) أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِآياتِ رَبِّهِمْ وَلِقائِهِ فَحَبِطَتْ أَعْمالُهُمْ ** (105) Those are the ones who deny the signs of their Lord and His encounter, so their works have failed. due to their disbelief, so they will not be rewarded for them. فَلا نُقِيمُ لَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ وَزْناً ** so We will not set up any weight for them on the Day of Resurrection. meaning We will scorn them and will not grant them any measure (مقداراً) or significance (اعتباراً), or We will not set up a balance (ميزاناً) for them by which their deeds are weighed, due to their failure (لانحباطها).

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): in a narration where he mentions the people of the Standing (أهل الموقف) and their states, among whom are the imams of disbelief (ائمّة الكفر) and the leaders of misguidance (قادة الضّلالة). So those are the ones فَلا نُقِيمُ لَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ وَزْناً ** (so We will not set up any weight for them on the Day of Resurrection), and no attention will be paid to them, because they paid no attention to His command and prohibition on the Day of Resurrection. So they are فِي جَهَنَّمَ خالِدُونَ تَلْفَحُ وُجُوهَهُمُ النَّارُ وَهُمْ فِيها كالِحُونَ.** (in Hell, abiding forever, the Fire will scorch their faces, and they will be grimacing therein.)

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): "Indeed, the fat man will come on the Day of Resurrection, not weighing the wing of a mosquito."

And al-Qummi said regarding وَزْناً ** (weight): meaning, a good deed (حسنة).

(١٠٦) ذلِكَ جَزاؤُهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ بِما كَفَرُوا وَاتَّخَذُوا آياتِي وَرُسُلِي هُزُواً (106) That is their requital: hell, because they were faithless and took My signs and My apostles in derision. He said: meaning the Successors (الأوصياء) are the signs (الآيات) which they took in derision. And in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) in what He wrote to al-Ma’mun: And it is obligatory to disassociate from the people of self-preference (اهل الاستيثار), such as Abu Musa al-Ash'ari and the people of his allegiance (أهل ولايته): الَّذِينَ ضَلَّ سَعْيُهُمْ فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا وَهُمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ يُحْسِنُونَ صُنْعاً أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِآياتِ رَبِّهِمْ (104) those whose endeavor has gone astray in the life of the world, while they suppose that they are doing good work. (105) They are those who deny the signs of their Lord by denying the guardianship (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). وَلِقائِهِ and His encounter. They disbelieved by meeting Allah without His Imamate (إمامته). فَحَبِطَتْ أَعْمالُهُمْ فَلا نُقِيمُ لَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ وَزْناً So their works have failed, and We will not set up any weight for them on the Day of Resurrection. So they are the dogs of the people of the Fire (أهل النّار).

(١٠٧) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ كانَتْ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتُ الْفِرْدَوْسِ نُزُلاً (107) Indeed those who have faith and do righteous deeds—the gardens of Paradise will be their hospitality,

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): The Garden (الجنّة) has one hundred degrees (درجة). The distance between every two degrees is like the distance between the heaven and the earth. al-Firdaws (الْفِرْدَوْسِ) is the highest degree of it. From it, the rivers of the Garden gush forth. So, when you ask Allah, ask Him for al-Firdaws (الْفِرْدَوْسِ).

And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (عليه السلام): This (verse) was revealed concerning Abu Dharr, al-Miqdad, Salman al-Farsi, and Ammar ibn Yasir. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, made for them the gardens of al-Firdaws (جَنَّاتُ الْفِرْدَوْسِ نُزُلاً), meaning: a refuge (مأوىً) and an abode (منزلاً).

(١٠٨) خالِدِينَ فِيها ** (They will be) abiding in it He said: They will not depart from it. لا يَبْغُونَ عَنْها حِوَلاً ** they will not seek any change from it. He said: They will not desire a substitute for it. (١٠٩) قُلْ لَوْ كانَ الْبَحْرُ مِداداً لِكَلِماتِ رَبِّي لَنَفِدَ الْبَحْرُ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَنْفَدَ كَلِماتُ رَبِّي ** Say, 'If the sea were ink for the words of my Lord, the sea would be exhausted sooner than the words of my Lord were exhausted,' And it was recited with the yā’ (بالياء). وَلَوْ جِئْنا بِمِثْلِهِ مَدَداً ** 'even if We brought another like it for supplement.' He said: Indeed, the speech of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has no end (آخر), no limit (غاية), and it will never cease. And مداد was recited with a kasrah on the mīm (بكسر الميم), being the plural of مدّة (مُدَّة), which is what the writer uses for ink (يستمدّ به الكاتب). It was said regarding the reason for its revelation (سَبب نزولها) what was mentioned previously in Surat Banī Isrā’īl concerning the Almighty's saying: وَما أُوتِيتُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً. and you have not been given of the knowledge except a little.

(١١٠) His saying, the Almighty: قُلْ إِنَّما أَنَا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُكُمْ ** (Say, 'I am only a human being like you) He said: meaning, in creation, that He is a created being like them, يُوحى إِلَيَّ أَنَّما إِلهُكُمْ إِلهٌ واحِدٌ** (who is inspired that your God is the One God.)

In al-Ihtijaj and Tafsir al-Imam (peace be upon him), regarding Surah al-Baqarah, he (peace be upon him) said concerning this verse: "Meaning, say to them: 'I am like you in humanity, but My Lord has singled Me out with prophethood, unlike you, just as He singles out some humans with wealth, health, and beauty, unlike other humans. So, do not deny that He may also single Me out with prophethood.'"

فَمَنْ كانَ يَرْجُوا لِقاءَ رَبِّهِ ** (So whoever expects to encounter his Lord,) believes that he will be resurrected. This is according to al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). فَلْيَعْمَلْ عَمَلاً صالِحاً ** (let him act righteously) purely for Allah **وَلا يُشْرِكْ بِعِبادَةِ رَبِّهِ أَحَداً ** (and not associate anyone with the worship of his Lord.)

Al-Qummi: This association (الشرك) is the association of showing off (رياء).

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله was asked about the interpretation of this verse, and he said: "Whoever prays to show off to people (مراياة النّاس) is an associate (مشرك), and whoever pays zakat to show off to people is an associate, and whoever fasts to show off to people is an associate, and whoever performs Hajj to show off to people is an associate, and whoever performs any deed that Allah has commanded to show off to people is an associate. And Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, does not accept the deed of one who shows off (مرائي)."

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) concerning this verse: "The man who performs a deed of reward (الثواب) not seeking the Face of Allah, but rather seeking the commendation (تزكية) of people, desiring that people hear about it—this is the one who has associated others with the worship of his Lord." Then he said: "There is no servant who conceals a good deed but that the days will pass until Allah reveals that good deed for him. And there is no servant who conceals an evil deed but that the days will pass until Allah reveals that evil deed for him."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man who performs a good deed, and a person sees it, and that pleases him. He said: "There is no harm. There is no one except that he loves that good be manifested for him among the people, provided he did not perform that deed for that reason."

And narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was performing ablution (يتوضّأ) for prayer, and a man wanted to pour water onto his hands, but he refused and recited this verse, saying: "And here I am performing ablution for prayer, which is worship (العبادة), and I dislike that anyone should associate with Me in it."

I say: This is another interpretation of the verse, and perhaps it is a matter of purification (تنزيه), whereas the former is a matter of prohibition (تحريم).

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the interpretation of this verse, and he said: "Whoever prays, or fasts, or emancipates a slave, or performs Hajj, desiring the praise (محمدة) of people, has associated others in his deed, and he is a forgiven associate (مشرك مغفور)."

I say: Meaning that it is not the association (الشرك) about which Allah the Almighty said: إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَغْفِرُ أَنْ يُشْرَكَ بِهِ ** (Indeed Allah does not forgive that partners should be ascribed to Him,) and that is because what is meant by that is the manifest association (الشرك الجليّ), whereas this is the hidden association (الشرك الخفيّ*).

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: "I am the wealthiest of partners, having no need of association (الشرك). So, whoever performs a deed in which he associates someone other than Me, I am free of him, and that deed belongs to the one he associated."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) who said: "Indeed, Allah says: 'I am the best partner. Whoever performs a deed for Me and for someone else, it belongs to the one for whom he performed it.'" And narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): "If a servant performs a deed seeking the mercy of Allah and the Hereafter, and then introduces into it the pleasure (رضا) of any person, he is an associate (مشركاً)."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and he said: "The righteous deed (العمل الصالح) is the recognition (المعرفة) of the Imams. وَلا يُشْرِكْ بِعِبادَةِ رَبِّهِ أَحَداً ** (and not associate anyone with the worship of his Lord) means submission (التّسليم) to Ali (peace be upon him), and not associating with him in the Caliphate (الخلافة) one who is not entitled to it, nor is he among its people."

And al-Qummi narrated from him (peace be upon him): وَلا يُشْرِكْ بِعِبادَةِ رَبِّهِ أَحَداً ** (and not associate anyone with the worship of his Lord). He said: "He should not take anyone besides the Wilayah (ولاية) of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) alongside them. And their Wilayah is the righteous deed (العمل الصالح). Whoever associates others with the worship of his Lord has associated others with our Wilayah, disbelieved in it, and denied the right and Wilayah of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him)."

In al-Faqih, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: "Whoever recites this verse upon going to sleep, قُلْ إِنَّما أَنَا بَشَرٌ (Say, 'I am only a human being) until the end of it, a light will shine forth for him from the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام), and that light will be filled with angels who seek forgiveness for him until morning."

And in Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "There is no servant who recites قُلْ إِنَّما أَنَا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُكُمْ ** (Say, 'I am only a human being like you) until the end of the Surah, except that he will have a light from his resting place to the Sacred House of Allah (بيت الله الحرام). And if he is among the people of the Sacred House of Allah, he will have a light extending to Bayt al-Maqdis (بيت المقدس)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "There is no servant who recites the end of al-Kahf upon sleeping except that he will wake up at the hour he desires."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites Surah al-Kahf every Friday night, it will be an expiation for what is between that Friday and the next Friday." He said: "And it is narrated that the same applies to whoever recites it on Friday after al-Zuhr (الظّهر) and al-Asr (العصر)."

And in Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites Surah al-Kahf every Friday night will not die except as a martyr (شهيداً), and Allah will resurrect him among the martyrs, and he will stand on the Day of Resurrection with the martyrs. O Allah, grant us its recitation, O Most Merciful of the merciful."

SURAH MARYAM

It is Makkan by consensus. Its number of verses is ninety-eight verses.

بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)
